name: herd init
description: Stand up herdkit in a project: scout (detects lang node/python/go/rust/java), interview, write .herd/config, seed .herd/healthcheck.project.sh from the template matching the detected stack, render coordinator skill
when_to_surface: Onboarding a new project to the herd workflow for the first time

name: herd doctor
description: One-pass dependency doctor: verify git, gh (+auth), claude, python3, herdr (+JSON contract) are present and working and that python3 can emit UTF-8 (Windows cp1252 guard, issue #31); reports every problem at once with per-platform install hints; soft deps (glow, shellcheck, bats) warn only; exits non-zero on any missing/broken hard dep. `herd doctor --posture` (HERD-154) adds a deterministic, no-LLM, report-only config-posture lint: it flags incoherent knob combos (a lever superseded/ignored by another key, each with a fix hint — e.g. legacy WATCHER_AUTOMERGE set alongside MERGE_POLICY) and reports whether the operator's effective posture is exercised by the posture-matrix sim (templates/postures.tsv); advisory, always exits 0
when_to_surface: When a herd command misbehaves, a fresh or Windows environment needs verifying, or the control room came up partially broken — run herd doctor to self-diagnose missing/broken dependencies in one pass; run herd doctor --posture to lint a custom .herd/config for incoherent or never-exercised knob combinations

name: herd upgrade
description: Bump HERD_VERSION and re-render the coordinator skill preserving all .herd/config answers
when_to_surface: After a herdkit engine update or when the coordinator template changes

name: herd render
description: Re-render only the coordinator skill from the current .herd/config without bumping version
when_to_surface: When only the template changed and no version bump is needed

name: herd map
description: Render docs/control-room-map.md — the operator-facing ONE-PAGE decision graph of how a change ships end-to-end: coordinator → lanes (feature/quick) → builder worktree → watcher gates (healthcheck + review) → verdict branches (PASS/BLOCK/refix) → merge policy → post-merge reconcile, plus bug routing (APP vs HERD-ENGINE), the async lanes (scribe/research), and the safety rails. The FLOW complement to `herd codemap` (the FILE map) and docs/driver-abstraction.md (the one runtime seam). Read-only + fail-soft: renders the committed doc with glow (themed) when available, else cat; distinct from codemap, which regenerates a scanned artifact rather than rendering a hand-authored one
when_to_surface: When an operator or new builder wants to orient on the whole ship-a-change pipeline in one page — the coordinator/lane/gate/verdict/merge/reconcile flow and where a bug or async task routes — instead of piecing it together from the code or the file map

name: herd codemap
description: Regenerate docs/codemap.md — a bash-native, DETERMINISTIC map of the herdkit engine tree scanned by scripts/herd/codemap.sh: module roles (bin/herd, each scripts/herd/*.sh, templates/ render surface, one line each from the file top comment), who-sources-whom (the static .-source edges between shell files), and config-key → consumer wiring (which script reads each kind=config key in this manifest). No timestamps/absolute paths, stable sort order, rewrites the file only when content changes (idempotent); prints what changed or 'up to date'
when_to_surface: When a coordinator/builder wants a compact, refreshable map of the codebase to load as context instead of re-exploring the repo each session, or after adding/renaming an engine script/config key so the committed map reflects the new tree

name: herd symbol-index
description: Regenerate docs/symbol-index.md — a bash-native, DETERMINISTIC function-level def→caller index of the herdkit engine tree scanned by scripts/herd/symbol-index.sh: for every shell function defined under bin/herd and scripts/herd/** (incl. backends/), its definition site(s) (file:line) and every CROSS-FILE call site. The function-level companion to `herd codemap` (which maps the same tree at FILE level): builders answer "who calls _backend_update_state" in one lookup instead of a grep-and-read session, and the coordinator sharpens its pre-spawn overlap read from file-level to symbol-level. No timestamps/absolute paths, stable sort order, rewrites the file only when content changes (idempotent); prints what changed or 'up to date'. HONEST SCOPE: a heuristic token scan, not a parser — it cannot bind a call to a specific same-named def, and command-position call detection can miss/over-count around dynamic dispatch, heredocs, strings, and inline comments (limits spelled out in the artifact header)
when_to_surface: When a coordinator/builder wants a def→caller map to jump straight to a function's definition or find its cross-file callers without a grep session, or after adding/renaming/moving an engine function so the committed index reflects the new symbol graph

name: herd ledger <set|get|list|rm|compact|path>
description: The COORDINATOR PROGRESS LEDGER (HERD-103): durable, cross-session coordinator-side state keyed by tracker id so a coordinator or a SECOND operator resumes with an accurate picture instead of re-deriving it from herdr/gh/backlog every session. Records per work item the coordinator facts nothing else holds: what was spawned (slug/worktree/PR/lane), sequencing decisions (seq/blocked-on notes), a planned marker for not-yet-spawned work, and a free-form status/note. APPEND-OR-UPDATE: `set <id> [k v]...` appends an update (later fields override earlier, so an update is just another line); `get <id> [--json]` folds to the item's current state; `list [--json] [--planned]` lists live items; `rm <id>` tombstones one out; `compact` folds history to one line per live item; `path` prints the file. Coordinator-side ONLY — NOT a watcher gate, nothing merges on it; it complements the engine journal (gate forensics) with the coordinator's editable plan+progress memory. Local + NEVER committed (WORKTREES_DIR/.herd/ledger.jsonl, gitignored); ZERO-SECRET by contract. Default-dormant: unused, no file exists and behavior is byte-identical
when_to_surface: At session start to reload prior coordinator state (list/get) and after each spawn or sequencing/planning decision to record it (set), so the picture survives a context reset or hand-off to another operator; never for watcher/gate decisions — it carries no gate authority

name: herd advise "<question>" [context…]
description: MID-FLIGHT strong-model ADVISOR (HERD-101): a builder pulls a ONE-SHOT second opinion on a hard decision from a strong advisor model (MODEL_ADVISE, default the MODEL_FEATURE/Opus tier) WITHOUT escalating its whole lane's tier, and reads the advice on stdout. Extra positional args after the question — and, when stdin is not a tty, piped stdin — are appended verbatim as context. No agent, no herdr tab, no repo mutation, no journal write: a pure one-shot query. ADDITIVE + FAIL-SOFT: an absent advisor model or a failed/empty model call degrades to a clear one-line 'unavailable' message on stdout and exit 0, never a hard error; a builder that never calls it sees byte-identical behavior
when_to_surface: When a builder on a cheaper tier hits ONE genuinely hard judgment call (an API shape, a tricky invariant, a which-design-is-safer fork) and wants a strong model's read without re-spawning the whole lane on Opus — it is a pull the builder chooses, not a push

name: herd reload
description: Rebuild the control room minus the coordinator: stop + relaunch the watcher with pane visibility verified, ensure the pinned backlog pane, re-render the coordinator skill; prints a per-component summary and effective MERGE_POLICY; never closes the coordinator tab/agent
when_to_surface: When .herd/config changes need to take effect, the watcher or backlog pane died or went invisible, or duplicate watchers must be cleared — safe to run from inside the coordinator

name: herd pane <watch|backlog|coordinator>
description: Restart ONE control-room pane in place without a full reload: watch stops + reruns the watcher in its registered pane (recreated below the coordinator if gone); backlog reruns the backlog viewer in its pane, interrupting a live viewer safely first; coordinator relaunches the coordinator agent pane (KILLS the live claude session, so it requires a typed 'yes' or --yes). Each rewrites .herd-panes from observed state and prints a per-component outcome; refuses when no project .herd/config resolves
when_to_surface: When just one pane died or went stale (watcher stopped, backlog viewer stuck, coordinator session needs a fresh start) and a full 'herd reload' is more than needed — surgically restart that single pane; use 'herd reload' instead when the whole control room needs rebuilding

name: herd update [--force]
description: Pull the herdkit engine (git pull --ff-only in HERDKIT_HOME), show the incoming delta, re-render the coordinator skill preserving .herd/config answers, and reload the workspace in one step; refuses if the engine checkout is dirty or builders are mid-flight (--force overrides both guards)
when_to_surface: When a fix has landed upstream in herdkit and you need to bring it in, or when the user asks 'how do I get the latest engine fix' — answer: run herd update

name: herd agent-update [--dry-run]
description: SAFELY update the AGENT RUNTIME the active driver points at (claude — or, via the driver seam, codex/grok), INSIDE the engine instead of a hand-run OS job. Opt-in via AGENT_UPDATE: off (default) is a HARD no-op (no probe, no exec, no xattr), byte-identical to before. When on it resolves the runtime binary + installer package names from the active driver's DRIVER_AGENT_BINARY/_NPM_PKG/_BREW_PKG/_NATIVE_UPDATE bindings, DETECTS the installer (brew formula|cask / npm global / native), runs the update, then STRIPS com.apple.quarantine from the RESOLVED binary on macOS — the issue #137 footgun where a `brew upgrade --cask` leaves the new binary quarantined so every new exec hangs in _dyld_start and spawned agents sit idle with blank panes. Distinct from `herd update` (which pulls the herdkit ENGINE, not the runtime). DRIVER-AWARE + FAIL-SOFT (a missing runtime or a failed installer command warns, never aborts); --dry-run prints every command it would run (installer + xattr) without executing
when_to_surface: When you want the engine to keep the agent runtime current instead of a hand-run brew/npm/native upgrade — especially on macOS, where a cask upgrade's quarantine flag silently wedges every spawned agent; enable with AGENT_UPDATE=on and preview with `herd agent-update --dry-run`

name: herd log [--pr N] [--tail]
description: Page the append-only engine journal (.herd/journal.jsonl): one readable line per gate event (review dispatch, verdict + provenance, healthcheck attempt/outcome, refix bounce, merge, reap, reload, infra death). --pr N filters to a single PR; --tail follows live
when_to_surface: When you want the raw chronological event stream, to watch gate activity live, or to see every recorded event for one PR before drilling in with herd why

name: herd why <pr#>
description: Summarize ONE PR's full gate history from the journal, chronologically: every dispatch, verdict (with reviewer/gate_default/infra provenance), healthcheck attempt + outcome, auto-refix bounce + wake result, and the merge/reap — the first post-mortem tool for 'what happened to this PR'
when_to_surface: FIRST tool to run when the user asks what happened to a PR, why a PR is stuck, or why a gate/review/merge failed — grounds the answer in recorded events instead of process archaeology

name: herd approve [<pr#|slug>] [--sha <sha>]
description: The APPROVAL front door under MERGE_POLICY=approve (HERD-200) — a first-class alias for scripts/herd/herd-approve.sh, which stays the single source of truth for the approval / override / push-hold / step-hold ledgers (bin/herd delegates verbatim, duplicating no logic). `herd approve <pr#>` approves the sha currently held for a gate-passed PR (--sha <sha> pins the approval to the commit the operator reviewed and REFUSES if a newer push superseded it); `herd approve <slug>` releases a pre-push (PUSH_GATE=human) or pipeline-step hold and resumes it; `herd approve list` (the default with no args) shows everything awaiting approval with its review verdict and any HUMAN-VERIFY steps; `herd approve why <pr#>` prints the verdict + block reason; `herd approve override <pr#>` records a human override of a cached BLOCK. A first arg that is not one of list/approve/why/override is treated as the argument of `approve`
when_to_surface: When a PR or builder is HELD awaiting a human — approve it, inspect why it is blocked, or override a cached BLOCK — without remembering the engine-script path; run `herd approve list` first to see what is waiting, then `herd approve <pr#>` to release it

name: herd status
description: One-shot READ-ONLY control-room health snapshot for THIS project (deterministic, no LLM, no mutation): (a) is the watcher alive? via the herd-watch-<workspace> argv0 marker / pid lock; (b) in-flight builders from herdr agent list joined to this project's worktrees, classified building/done/idle and flagged DEAD when a worktree has no live agent + no open PR + no commits (a small read-only replica of the watcher's dead signature); (c) open PRs + gate state (mergeable/mergeStateStatus + last review/health verdict from the watcher's ledgers + GitHub reviewDecision); (d) backlog open/in-progress counts. Exits NON-ZERO when something needs attention (DEAD builder, CONFLICTING PR, review-blocked PR), 0 when healthy
when_to_surface: When you (or a cron/dashboard) want a fast, greppable 'is this control room healthy right now?' answer for one project without opening the live TUI — surfaces dead builders, conflicting or review-blocked PRs, and whether the watcher is even running; the single-project analogue of 'herd fleet status'

name: herd sweep
description: ONE-COMMAND CONTROL-ROOM CLEANUP (HERD-191): runs every reaper the engine owns, on demand, composing the shipped helpers rather than reimplementing them (sweep.sh loads agent-watch.sh via AGENT_WATCH_LIB=1). Five legs: (1) WORKTREES — sha-anchored reap of merged/closed-PR worktrees (a PR whose headRefOid equals the tree's HEAD; regenerable dirt like .DS_Store/__pycache__ is tolerated) plus unowned tmp/detached scratch trees that carry ZERO unique commits (a detached HEAD's commits are reachable from no branch ref, so removing the worktree destroys them irrecoverably — such a tree is FLAGGED, never reaped); (2) TABS — stale engine tabs, registry-allowlisted + workspace-scoped, never another project's; (3) MARKERS — dead-pid inflight markers via HERD-185's restart-safe corpse sweep (pid + start-time recycling guard), which also frees the gate slot and counts the retry budget; (4) PROCESSES — orphaned (ppid=1) bats/healthcheck trees attributed to THIS project by path-boundary match, killed by process group after a start-time re-verify, listed before they die; a live inflight marker's worker is exempt (a backgrounded gate worker is ppid=1 but still collectable), and duplicate argv0-tagged watchers are reported; (5) WATCHER — restart the watcher pane via cmd_pane_watch (the single watcher-killing code path, which also reaps the duplicates) then verify it survives one tick. SAFETY: anything not PROVABLY disposable — a worktree with real dirt, or ANY tree (closed-PR branch or detached scratch HEAD) carrying commits that exist nowhere else — is FLAGGED WITH EVIDENCE and NEVER deleted, in any mode. --dry-run prints the full plan and touches nothing; --no-restart skips leg 5. Every action is journaled
when_to_surface: After a watcher crash, an overnight merge burst, or any session that left debris — stale tabs, worktrees whose PR merged hours ago, inflight-marker corpses holding a gate slot, orphaned bats/healthcheck trees. Run 'herd sweep --dry-run' first to read the plan, then 'herd sweep' to execute. The watcher also detects the same debris on its own cadence and renders a '🧹 sweep recommended' row (see SWEEP_AUTO)

name: herd cost [--pr N]
description: Aggregate the engine journal's token/cost events (measured per builder and per in-worktree review at merge time, deduped by message id and priced against the perishable model→$ table): per-PR cost, cost-per-merged-PR, and running totals by component and model; --pr N drills into one PR's cost lines. READ-ONLY measurement precursor to the efficiency program — never changes any gate
when_to_surface: When the user asks how many tokens or how much $ a builder/review/PR cost, wants cost-per-merged-PR, or is scoping the flow-audit / efficiency levers and needs the measured spend instead of a guess

name: herd stats [--today|--since <date>|--pr N]
description: A shepherd's digest CARD: a compact, DETERMINISTIC (zero-LLM) rollup of a window of the engine journal (.herd/journal.jsonl), same reader pattern as herd cost. Aggregates PRs merged (a 🐑 each), review verdicts grouped by value AND provenance (reviewer/gate_default/infra), refix bounces, limit park (limit_detected) / resume (limit_resume_result), reaps, and recorded cost. No filter = all time; --today and --since <date> bound by the ISO ts (string compare, lexical); --pr N scopes to one PR; filters compose (AND). Fail-soft: an empty or missing journal prints an all-zeros card, never an error; no LLM, no mutation
when_to_surface: When you want a fast throughput+friction snapshot of the herd over a window — how many PRs shipped today, how the review verdicts split and where they came from, how much refix/limit/reap churn happened, and the cost — without reading the raw stream (herd log) or drilling one PR (herd why)

name: herd backlog
description: List open work items via the active SCRIBE_BACKEND (file/github/linear) in a uniform line shape; subcommands: --rich (state+desc TSV), show <#id>, browse (fzf), and the HERD-52 planned-work markers — queued (list plan-time markers, >24h flagged ADVISORY), queue <#id> [--after <blocker>] (publish 'I'm building this next' so a second operator doesn't grab it), unqueue <#id> (clear it). queue/unqueue/queued are backend-optional + fail-soft
when_to_surface: Any time the coordinator needs the current open work set regardless of backend, or (queued/queue/unqueue) to check/publish plan-time intent before picking or spawning an item

name: herd backend switch
description: Guided work-tracker backend switch (file|github|linear|jira|changelog): preflights credentials BEFORE any change (linear prompts for the API key on a tty → .herd/secrets chmod 600 + gitignore, then proves it with a live viewer{} round-trip; jira prompts for JIRA_BASE_URL/JIRA_EMAIL/JIRA_API_TOKEN → .herd/secrets, then proves it with a live /myself round-trip; github checks gh auth), flips SCRIBE_BACKEND via the validated config-set path (inherits the #139 stale-drainer warning + skill re-render), optionally --migrate replays the old backend's open items into the new one (idempotent — already-present titles skipped, '(migrated from …)' provenance suffix), stamps BACKLOG.md with a frozen-archive banner when leaving the file backend, restarts the backlog viewer pane, and journals backend_switched
when_to_surface: When the operator asks to move work tracking between a markdown file, GitHub Issues, Linear, or Jira — run this ONE command instead of hand-editing config/secrets; everything downstream (scribe drainer, herd backlog, watcher mark-shipped, coordinator skill) follows SCRIBE_BACKEND automatically

name: herd report [--to <link>] [--dep] "<symptom>"
description: File a cross-repo issue on HERD_REPO or a peer from .herd/links via --to; deduplicates first. Default is fire-and-forget dispatch; add --dep (requires --to) to ALSO record the filed item as a blocked-on dependency in .herd/deps so dep-watcher tracks it
when_to_surface: When a HERD-ENGINE bug needs to escalate to the herdkit repo or a registered peer; use --dep when this lane cannot proceed until the filed item is resolved

name: herd link list
description: List peer repos registered in .herd/links with their backend and routing target
when_to_surface: When reviewing cross-repo filing targets or confirming link names for herd report --to

name: herd depend <link>#<id>
description: Record a WATCHED blocked-on dependency on a peer-repo item (link resolved via .herd/links) as a blocked-on row in .herd/deps; dep-watcher.sh polls it until the upstream item closes, then notifies and un-blocks. Backward-compatible with the row format the watcher already parses
when_to_surface: When a feature cannot ship until a specific upstream item (a peer-repo issue/PR) closes — records the blocker as tracked, watched state rather than fire-and-forget

name: herd deps <list|rm|demote>
description: Inspect and edit recorded dependencies — a dep is editable data, never stuck: list prints each dep with its current upstream state; rm drops one (warns if a blocker is still open); demote reclassifies a blocked-on into a non-blocking watch row (instantly un-blocks the lane while keeping the item on the radar)
when_to_surface: When reviewing what this project is blocked on, dropping a resolved/obsolete dep, or downgrading a hard blocker to a non-blocking watch so the lane can proceed

name: herd config <list|get|set>
description: View/validate/set .herd/config keys with the known-key set + per-key metadata sourced from templates/capabilities.tsv (this manifest); set edits in place preserving comments then restarts the watcher (watcher-affecting keys) or re-renders the coordinator skill (coordinator-facing keys). `set --local` (HERD-47) routes the write to the gitignored per-user overlay .herd/config.local; `set --shared` (HERD-62) applies a PROJECT-scoped change to the committed baseline AND opens a tiny single-file PR (branch config/<key>, body stating old->new and an optional --reason) so operators who cannot push to the default branch still propagate policy the gated way — others receive it via `git pull` + `herd update`. --shared is refused for machine-scoped keys and is mutually exclusive with --local. Never touches DENY_PATHS or .herd/secrets; masks secret-shaped values
when_to_surface: Anytime to inspect or change a workflow preference post-init (nothing is init-only); use --shared when a PROJECT-scoped policy change (CONTEXT_PROVISION, MERGE_POLICY, CLAIM_REQUIRED, TRACKED_SPAWNS, ...) must reach every operator; the coordinator's Workflow settings menu drives it

name: herd theme <list|preview|set>
description: The active-THEME picker (HERD-145): a thin operator surface over the pluggable-theming seam (scripts/herd/theme.sh) that never re-implements resolution. `list` prints the catalog — every engine BUILT-IN under templates/themes/ (tokyonight, gruvbox, catppuccin, nord) plus every USER theme under .herd/themes/<name>/ — marking the ACTIVE one (current HERD_THEME) and noting any built-in shadowed by a user theme of the same name. `preview [<name>]` renders that theme's palette as color swatches (default: the active theme). `set <name>` validates the name resolves to a COMPLETE theme dir (palette.sh + glow.json, user or built-in) then writes HERD_THEME through the validated `herd config set` path (manifest validation + the watcher restart that repaints every live surface), refusing an unknown/incomplete theme rather than persisting a value that would silently fall back to tokyonight. Fail-soft + NO_COLOR / non-TTY aware: swatches degrade to plain rows
when_to_surface: When switching the herd color theme or browsing available themes: run `herd theme list` to see built-ins + user themes, `herd theme preview <name>` to compare palettes, and `herd theme set <name>` to apply one (a project's own theme drops palette.sh + glow.json into .herd/themes/<name>/ first)

name: herd governance <export|apply>
description: Export/apply a project's GOVERNANCE as a portable, versioned profile (HERD-126). `herd governance export [--file <path>]` writes the governance-scoped .herd/config key subset (every key marked governance in this manifest — merge/gate/PR/attribution/commit policy; secrets and machine-scoped keys NEVER travel) as a commented, versioned profile to <path> or stdout. `herd governance apply <file>` PROPOSES each key interactively (evidence = the profile line, current->proposed) via the VALIDATED `herd config set` — never a blind overwrite; --yes applies all non-interactively (journaled) for automation; declining every key leaves .herd/config untouched. A malformed profile (missing/unknown version marker, an unknown/non-governance/secret/machine key, or a shell-active value) is refused LOUDLY with ZERO partial writes. Profiles are ONE optional governance source among several — apply composes with the interview, `herd init --governance <file>` seeding, and manual `herd config set`; `herd fleet set --profile <file>` propagates a profile across the whole registry
when_to_surface: When you want to capture a project's governance stance (merge policy, push gate, PR flow, commit/attribution conventions) as a portable artifact to review, version-control, seed a fresh install with (herd init --governance <file>), or roll out across several repos (herd fleet set --profile <file>); export it, then apply it elsewhere

name: herd fleet <register|list|discover|status|digest|inbox|upgrade|reload>
description: DETERMINISTIC (no-LLM) multi-project fan-out over a flat project registry (default ~/.herd/fleet, one line per project: name|path|repo): register <path> adds a herd project (read from its .herd/config); list prints the registry; discover [--register] <root>... scans roots for .herd/config projects; status prints a read-only per-project rollup (branch, open PR count, watcher alive? via the herd-watch-<workspace> argv0 marker, last .herd/journal.jsonl activity); digest [--since <dur>] aggregates every project's .herd/journal.jsonl over a window (default 24h) into a cross-project standup (per project: shipped/merged, needs-you holds+escalations, blocked BLOCK verdicts, in-flight reviews, gate-failure count) plus a fleet summary line; inbox is the cross-project ATTENTION INBOX — one view of what needs the human RIGHT NOW, joining each project's journal-derived CURRENT state (human-verify/approval holds, review BLOCK verdicts, escalations, CODEERROR health gates; a released hold or merged PR clears itself) with gh's live CONFLICTING PRs, printing each item as PR# · reason · suggested action (herd why <pr> / herd-approve.sh approve <pr> / herd-resolve.sh <slug>) plus a fleet-wide count, a clean project shown as clean; upgrade and reload DELEGATE to each project's own 'herd update'/'herd reload' and collect a per-project outcome table. Read-mostly — never mutates a project's tree beyond what the delegated per-project command already does; a missing/dirty/gh-unavailable project is reported, not fatal
when_to_surface: When managing several herd projects at once — bulk-upgrade or reload every repo, or get one cross-project status board (which watchers are alive, what each is doing, open PRs), a daily digest/standup of what shipped/blocked/is-in-flight, or one attention inbox of what needs you right now (blocked PRs, holds, conflicts, failed gates) across all repos, instead of visiting each control room; P0+P1+P2 of the Master/fleet-coordinator EPIC

name: herd governance hooks render [--shared]
description: Render SESSION-TIME Claude Code hooks from the governance map's surface==hook rows (HERD-131). Dual-surface enforcement: the same governance rules the merge-time watcher gate enforces ALSO bind at the keyboard as PreToolUse hooks (e.g. block a `git commit` carrying an AI attribution trailer — the session sibling of the ATTRIBUTION_POLICY merge-time lint; warn on a direct `git push` under PUSH_GATE=human). PROPOSE-then-write: each hook is shown and the operator accepts per-hook; accepted entries MERGE into .claude/settings.local.json (default) or .claude/settings.json (--shared), preserving every existing entry. Each generated matcher-group carries a herd-governance marker key so re-render strips ONLY its own prior entries and re-adds the current selection (idempotent). Fail-soft: no map / no hook rows / non-interactive → clean no-op, no file written
when_to_surface: After importing governance from a CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md (herd init adoption, HERD-119) or setting gates manually — bind the hook-surface rules session-time so a violation is blocked at the keyboard, not only at merge; safe to re-run to refresh

name: fleet.sh
description: Helper library behind 'herd fleet' (sourced by bin/herd): the project-registry reader/writer, per-project config resolver, status rollup (branch/open-PRs/watcher-liveness/last-journal-activity), and the update/reload fan-out that delegates to each project's own herd command
when_to_surface: Internal to 'herd fleet'; not launched directly

name: herd-feature.sh
description: Full feature lane: creates worktree, opens live app preview pane, spawns Claude agent, runs healthcheck before PR
when_to_surface: App-facing changes where a running preview aids development; APP_PREVIEW_CMD must be set

name: herd-quick.sh
description: Lightweight lane: creates worktree, single agent pane, no preview, runs healthcheck before PR
when_to_surface: Trivial or non-app changes: scripts, docs, config, engine fixes; no APP_PREVIEW_CMD required

name: herd-resolve.sh
description: Isolated conflict resolver: merges default branch into worktree, fixes conflicts, verifies, pushes or escalates
when_to_surface: When the watcher surfaces a CONFLICTING PR that cannot auto-resolve

name: herd-approve.sh
description: Approval entry-point for MERGE_POLICY=approve AND per-PR human-verify holds: list gate-passed PRs (printing any declared HUMAN-VERIFY steps) and write sha-keyed approval records
when_to_surface: When MERGE_POLICY=approve, or a PR declared a HUMAN-VERIFY block, and it has passed all gates but needs coordinator sign-off before the watcher merges

name: push-gate.sh
description: PUSH_GATE=human hold helper (HERD-123): a finished builder records a sha-keyed pre-push hold instead of pushing; herd-approve.sh approve <slug> resumes push + PR creation. Sourced by herd-approve.sh (list/approve/resume) and agent-watch.sh (the 'ready · awaiting push approval' console row); run directly by the builder lane (hold)
when_to_surface: Only reached when PUSH_GATE=human; a builder holds here before its first push and a human approves the LOCAL diff to resume

name: governance-hook.sh
description: Session-time governance enforcer (HERD-131): the command rendered into a project's .claude/settings by `herd governance hooks render`. Invoked by Claude Code as a PreToolUse hook — reads the tool payload on stdin, extracts the Bash command, and enforces ONE governance rule at the keyboard (exit 2 = block, exit 0 = allow/warn). Complements the merge-time gate (dual-surface). Targets: pre-commit:no-ai-coauthor (block an AI attribution trailer), pre-push:human-gate (warn under PUSH_GATE=human), pre-action:run-checks, pre-action:format. Unknown target → fail-open
when_to_surface: Not run directly — Claude Code invokes it per the settings.json hook entry the render command writes; run standalone only to pipe-test a rule

name: steps.sh
description: Pipeline steps runner (HERD-132): runs operator-defined stages from .herd/steps.tsv at the post-build | post-healthcheck | pre-merge | post-merge seams — a shell command or a skill:<name> invoked in the builder session. on_fail=block|warn; hold=none|notify|approve (approve reuses the sha-keyed herd-approve ledger). Sourced by herd-feature.sh / herd-quick.sh (builder-seam rule), agent-watch.sh (pre/post-merge seams in do_merge), and herd-approve.sh (list/approve/release a step hold); run directly as a CLI (steps.sh run <seam> | validate | holds). Every step journals a step_run event; an absent/empty step list is a byte-identical pipeline
when_to_surface: Only reached when .herd/steps.tsv defines steps; steps ADD checks at the seams and never bypass the built-in review/healthcheck floor — an operator plugs their own review skill / doc-pass / checkpoint in as a stage

name: triggers.sh
description: Scheduled/triggered runs (HERD-169): a cron-style trigger in .herd/triggers.tsv spawns a defined workflow (a named backlog-item template or a steps.tsv pipeline named in its task text) on schedule by ENQUEUEING intents onto the EXISTING durable spawn queue via spawn.sh — it never touches the queue internals. DIFF-AGAINST-LAST-RUN: each fire diffs the trigger's input set against a per-trigger snapshot under .herd/trigger-state/ and spawns ONLY the delta; first run (no snapshot) = a loudly-labelled full run; an input-command error is fail-soft (spawns nothing, keeps the snapshot). Run as a CLI (triggers.sh tick [--now EPOCH] [--force] | fire <name> | validate | list). An absent/empty triggers.tsv is a byte-identical no-op (ships dormant)
when_to_surface: Only reached when .herd/triggers.tsv defines triggers; the tick cadence is an operator cron/scheduler and the schedule column decides which triggers are due — every spawned intent still runs the normal build/healthcheck/review/merge pipeline

name: herd-approve.sh why <pr#>
description: Print the latest review verdict and block reason from the review ledger and PR comment
when_to_surface: When a PR shows 'review blocked' in the watch console and the coordinator needs to understand why before deciding to fix or override

name: herd-approve.sh override <pr#>
description: Record a sha-keyed human override so the watcher treats a cached BLOCK as passed for the PR's current commit
when_to_surface: When the coordinator decides to override a review block; a new commit invalidates the override and triggers re-review

name: dep-watcher.sh
description: Polls .herd/deps for blocked-on upstream PRs; transitions items to ready when the upstream PR merges
when_to_surface: When .herd/deps exists and features are waiting on a peer-repo PR to merge first

name: coordinator.sh
description: Launches the two-pane herd control room: backlog viewer pane and agent-watch status pane
when_to_surface: To start or restart the herd control room for a project

name: research.sh
description: Enqueue a research question on the async research lane; returns REQ_ID instantly without blocking
when_to_surface: When the coordinator needs to look into the repo without occupying its own context window

name: research-get.sh
description: Fetch a completed research report by REQ_ID; prints PENDING if the drainer has not filed it yet
when_to_surface: After enqueueing research with research.sh, to retrieve the result when needed

name: scribe.sh
description: Enqueue a backlog change; a single async drainer applies it with git pull plus targeted edit so no clobber occurs
when_to_surface: Any time the coordinator updates the backlog — never edit BACKLOG_FILE directly

name: spawn.sh
description: Enqueue a builder spawn intent (slug + lane=quick|feature + task text) to the durable spawn queue ($WORKTREES_DIR/spawn-queue/) and return instantly with an INTENT_ID. The watcher drains the queue each tick via spawn-step.sh, launching herd-quick.sh or herd-feature.sh bounded by REVIEW_CONCURRENCY + SPAWN_AHEAD — so a coordinator pause never loses a spawn and the pipeline cap is always respected
when_to_surface: When the coordinator wants to launch a new builder without blocking its own context window; preferred over calling herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh directly when throughput matters

name: spawn-step.sh
description: Atomic queue mechanics for the durable spawn queue, called from agent-watch.sh's _drain_spawn_queue (NOT a Claude drainer). Subcommands: next reclaims stale claims and atomically claims the oldest pending intent (prints CLAIMED + slug/lane/task or EMPTY, returns immediately); done removes a claimed file after a successful launch; skip logs a warning and drops a malformed intent without crashing the watcher
when_to_surface: Internal mechanics of the spawn queue drain; not called directly by the coordinator

name: backlog-reconcile.sh
description: Reconcile BACKLOG.md against a merged PR's rename/move surface: diff the PR (surface = renamed/moved/deleted files via git rename detection + functions/section-headers defined before the PR and gone after), find backlog entries that still reference the old path/name, and enqueue ONE scribe request to update or ⚠️-flag each dangling entry (subcommands: surface | scan | run <pr#|git-range>); never edits the backlog itself
when_to_surface: After landing a cutover/extraction/rename PR that moves files, renames functions, or retitles sections the backlog points at — run `backlog-reconcile.sh run <pr#>` so stale references get fixed or flagged instead of dangling silently

name: backlog-reconcile-sweep.sh
description: Periodic ADVISORY drift sweep: cross-reference open 🔜 backlog items against recently-merged PRs (gh pr list --state merged) and recent commit subjects (git log) by conservative key-term overlap, and PRINT a ranked list of probably-shipped-but-still-🔜 candidates (title + matching PR#/commit + confidence). Catches work that shipped via an automated path (auto-refix / direct hand-off) without being reaped. Default is report-only and never edits the backlog; --enqueue files ONE scribe reconcile request per HIGH-confidence candidate (the scribe verifies before touching BACKLOG.md)
when_to_surface: Run periodically (or when the backlog looks stale after a merge burst) to catch 🔜 items that already shipped — review the printed candidates, then re-run with --enqueue to have the scribe reap the confirmed ones

name: governance-drift-sweep.sh
description: Periodic ADVISORY governance-DRIFT sweep (HERD-125): re-extract an OPTIONAL CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md with the SAME deterministic extraction the HERD-119 init adoption pass uses (scripts/herd/governance.sh + templates/governance-map.tsv) and DIFF the config-key rules the prose maps to against the effective .herd/config. A mapped rule whose value differs from the enforced config is DRIFT — surfaced LOUDLY (a stdout advisory naming the key + proposed value + the exact `herd config set` to adopt, one governance_drift journal event per drifted key, and with --pr N one PR comment) but NEVER auto-applied. Fail-soft and BYTE-IDENTICALLY SILENT when there is no such file or every mapped rule already matches the config. Reuses HERD-119 extraction (same file surface, which is why HERD-125 is sequenced after it)
when_to_surface: Run periodically (or after a CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md edit lands on the default branch) to catch governance PROSE that drifted from the enforced .herd/config — the advisory names each key and the `herd config set` to adopt it; add --pr N to also comment on a PR. Advisory only: it never edits config, so review and adopt (or update the prose) by hand

name: agent-watch.sh
description: Live status console and auto-merge state machine: polls PRs, runs healthcheck and review gate, merges ready PRs; a PR whose body declares a HUMAN-VERIFY block is held for sha-keyed approval instead of auto-merging
when_to_surface: Runs continuously in the watcher pane; coordinator checks its verdicts for blocked, held, or failed PRs

name: herd-review.sh
description: Adversarial pre-merge correctness gate invoked by the watcher before any merge
when_to_surface: Automatically invoked by agent-watch.sh; coordinator surfaces CODE failures back to the sub-agent

name: PROJECT_ROOT
description: Absolute path to the project git root; all scripts derive paths from this value
when_to_surface: Always required; set by herd init; must point to the repo root

name: WORKTREES_DIR
description: Directory where feature worktrees are created; must be a sibling of PROJECT_ROOT and never committed
when_to_surface: Always required; must exist outside the project repo before lanes are used

name: DEFAULT_BRANCH
description: Remote/branch to fork from and merge PRs into (e.g. origin/main)
when_to_surface: Always required; controls PR targets and the scribe git pull/push destination

name: BRANCH_TEMPLATE
description: Project-defined branch naming for the builder lanes (HERD-120): the template the lanes' feature branches are rendered from (default: feat/{slug} — byte-identical to the historically-hardcoded feat/<slug>, so an unset key is zero behavior change). Tokens: {slug} (required, the coordinator-chosen kebab name) and optional {ref} (the tracker id, e.g. HERD-120 — filled when the coordinator spawns from a tracked item). ONE shared render+parse helper (herd_branch_render / herd_branch_parse in herd-config.sh) routes EVERY branch construction (new-feature.sh) and parse site (watcher dep-check + orphan-tab sweep) through it so naming stays consistent end-to-end. FAIL-SOFT: a malformed template (missing {slug}) warns and falls back to the default rather than producing an unusable branch
when_to_surface: Set to rename the lanes' feature branches to a project convention (e.g. '{ref}/{slug}' or 'wip/{slug}'); leave unset (default feat/{slug}) for byte-identical legacy naming

name: WORKSPACE_NAME
description: Human-readable project label used in coordinator title, herdr agent names, and singleton identifiers
when_to_surface: Always required; two projects sharing one herdr instance must have distinct names

name: BACKLOG_FILE
description: Repo-relative path to the backlog file (default: BACKLOG.md)
when_to_surface: Used by the file and changelog backends; can be omitted when SCRIBE_BACKEND=github or linear

name: SHIPPED_KEEP
description: How many most-recent entries the file backend keeps in BACKLOG.md's '## Recently shipped' section before older ones are moved to BACKLOG.archive.md (backends/file.sh). Default 10.
when_to_surface: Raise to keep more shipped items inline; lower to archive sooner.

name: JOURNAL_MAX_MB
description: Journal self-rotation threshold in MB (journal.sh): before each append, a journal grown past this is rotated to journal-<stamp>.jsonl (JOURNAL_MAX_BYTES is a test-seam override). Default 10.
when_to_surface: Raise for a high-volume journal; lower to rotate sooner.

name: JOURNAL_KEEP_DAYS
description: How many days of rotated journal archives to retain (journal.sh); older journal-<stamp>.jsonl files are pruned. Default 30.
when_to_surface: Raise to keep more journal history; lower to prune sooner.

name: SCRIBE_BACKEND
description: Work-tracker backend adapter: file | github | linear | jira | changelog
when_to_surface: Set when items are tracked in GitHub Issues, Linear, or Jira instead of a markdown file

name: SCRIBE_LINGER_SECS
description: Backlog-drainer linger window in seconds (HERD-88): after the scribe empties the queue, `scribe-step.sh next` keeps polling this many extra seconds before returning EMPTY so the drainer session stays alive across idle gaps between requests, letting a burst share ONE MODEL_SCRIBE session instead of paying a fresh cold-start per gap. Default 0 → today's behavior byte-identical (next's total wait stays == SCRIBE_POLL); suggest 90. A request enqueued during the linger is claimed by the same session; single-drainer mkdir-mutex semantics are unchanged
when_to_surface: Set to cut scribe cold-start cost when backlog updates arrive in bursts with short gaps (e.g. a merge burst enqueuing several reconciles); leave 0 for byte-identical legacy behavior

name: SCRIBE_POLL
description: Backlog-drainer base poll window in seconds (scribe-step.sh): how long `scribe-step.sh next` waits for a queued request before returning empty; SCRIBE_LINGER_SECS extends this across idle gaps in a burst. Default 25.
when_to_surface: Raise to reduce scribe cold-starts on a bursty queue; lower for snappier drain latency.

name: RESEARCH_POLL
description: Research-drainer base poll window in seconds (research-step.sh): how long `research-step.sh next` waits for a queued research request before returning empty. Default 25.
when_to_surface: Raise to reduce researcher cold-starts on a bursty queue; lower for snappier latency.

name: DRAINER_HEARTBEAT_TIMEOUT
description: Drainer singleton liveness window in seconds (HERD-109). The scribe and researcher drainers are per-project singletons: an enqueue that finds a same-named drainer in `herdr agent list` normally short-circuits and spawns nothing — a liveness blind spot, since a LISTED but HUNG drainer (wedged claude session / stuck step) then blocks the queue forever. When set, scribe-step.sh / research-step.sh heartbeat on every drain step (and each poll iteration); if the enqueue path finds a running drainer whose heartbeat is staler than this, it treats it as HUNG, RECLAIMS the singleton and spawns a FRESH drainer. The queue's atomic per-request claim (`.req`→`.req.mine`) prevents double-draining. Default 900 (15 min) — far above any legitimate single drain step, so a healthy drainer is never falsely reclaimed and behavior is byte-identical; 0 DISABLES liveness reclaim (pure legacy behavior); non-numeric → off. An ABSENT heartbeat is treated as alive (fail-soft)
when_to_surface: Leave at the 900 default in almost all cases; lower it only if a hung drainer must be reclaimed faster (at more risk of reclaiming a legitimately slow drainer), or set 0 to disable liveness reclaim entirely

name: BACKLOG_VIEW_EXTRAS
description: View-only backlog-pane extra section: "" (default, off → pane byte-identical to today) | github-issues. When github-issues, the backlog viewer renders a SECOND, clearly-labeled '📥 incoming (github issues)' section BENEATH the primary work queue listing this repo's open GitHub issues (the herd-report incoming inbox) via `gh issue list`. Strictly ADDITIVE display: never merged into the primary list, never feeds `herd backlog` or coordinator work-selection (SCRIBE_BACKEND stays the single source of truth); fails SOFT on any gh error with a quiet 'incoming unavailable' note, never a red row. Applied on the backlog pane's next launch (herd pane backlog / herd reload)
when_to_surface: Set to surface this repo's open GitHub issues as an incoming inbox beneath the live work queue, without letting them enter planned-work selection

name: SHARE_LINKS
description: Space-separated dirs to symlink into each new worktree (e.g. node_modules target)
when_to_surface: When worktrees need large shared dirs that should not be duplicated on disk

name: MODEL_COORDINATOR
description: Claude model for the coordinator agent (default: claude-opus-4-8)
when_to_surface: Override to trade cost for quality on coordination tasks

name: MODEL_FEATURE
description: Claude model for full feature lane agents (eco-leaning starter default: claude-sonnet-4-6; Opus is now an ESCALATION tier reached via MODEL_ESCALATE_GLOB, not a default)
when_to_surface: Override when a different model tier is preferred for feature implementation; an explicit key always beats the starter default

name: MODEL_QUICK
description: Claude model for quick lane agents (eco-leaning starter default: claude-haiku-4-5, the cheapest tier)
when_to_surface: Override for lightweight task agents; an explicit key always beats the starter default

name: MODEL_SCRIBE
description: Claude model for the async backlog scribe drainer (default: claude-sonnet-4-6)
when_to_surface: Override for backlog editing tasks

name: MODEL_RESEARCH
description: Claude model for the async researcher drainer (default: claude-sonnet-4-6)
when_to_surface: Override for repo research tasks

name: MODEL_REVIEW
description: Claude model for the adversarial pre-merge review gate (eco-leaning starter default: claude-sonnet-4-6; Opus is now an ESCALATION tier reached via REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB, not a default)
when_to_surface: Override when a different model tier is preferred for correctness review; an explicit key always beats the starter default

name: MODEL_RESOLVER
description: Claude model for the isolated conflict-resolver agent (default: claude-sonnet-4-6)
when_to_surface: Override for conflict resolution; resolvers do mechanical merge work, so a cheaper tier is usually fine

name: MODEL_ADVISE
description: Claude model for the `herd advise` one-shot mid-flight advisor (HERD-101): the strong tier a builder pulls a second opinion from without escalating its whole lane (default: the resolved MODEL_FEATURE tier — Opus under standard TOKEN_MODE, sonnet under eco). Set explicitly to pin the advisor to a specific strong model regardless of the feature tier; an explicit key always beats the default
when_to_surface: Override to point the mid-flight advisor at a different (usually strong) model than the feature lane uses, or to keep it on Opus while the feature lane runs a cheaper tier

name: TOKEN_MODE
description: Token-budget mode: standard (default) | eco. eco flips the BUILT-IN model defaults to cheaper tiers (coordinator/feature→claude-sonnet-4-6; quick/scribe/research/resolver→claude-haiku-4-5; review→claude-sonnet-4-6). An explicit MODEL_* key in .herd/config ALWAYS beats eco — eco replaces built-in defaults only, never a user override
when_to_surface: Opt in to cut engine token cost. Tradeoffs: the review gate on sonnet may miss subtle logic bugs; the feature builder on sonnet is weaker on complex multi-file work; haiku is fine for mechanical scribe/resolver work. Composes with the model step-up item, which escalates FROM whatever tier eco sets

name: APP_PREVIEW_CMD
description: Shell command to launch the live app preview pane (blank = no preview pane)
when_to_surface: For projects with a runnable app; blank restricts lanes to herd-quick.sh only

name: APP_PREVIEW_SERVER_ARGS
description: Extra launch args appended to $APP_PREVIEW_CMD for the preview server, with a literal {port} token substituted by the chosen port (default: --server.port {port} --server.headless true, reproducing today's dev-server flags exactly); set "" for a command that needs no injected flags (it reads $PORT itself or takes the port another way)
when_to_surface: Set for a non-web-framework preview command (a CLI, a Go/Node/Rust server, java -jar) that the default dev-server flags would crash; leave unset to keep an existing web app byte-for-byte unchanged

name: APP_PREVIEW_HEALTH_CMD
description: Preview health-probe command, run with PORT exported; exit 0 = 🟢 serving. Highest-precedence probe: overrides APP_PREVIEW_HEALTH_PATH when set; empty (default) falls through to the HTTP path probe
when_to_surface: Set for a CLI / gRPC / non-HTTP or non-root-path preview that a curl GET / can never verify

name: APP_PREVIEW_HEALTH_PATH
description: HTTP path curled at http://localhost:$PORT<path> for the preview health probe; HTTP 200 = 🟢 serving (default: /, reproducing today's probe). Set "" to DISABLE the HTTP probe — with no APP_PREVIEW_HEALTH_CMD either, health renders ⚪ unknown, never a false-red 🔴
when_to_surface: Set a non-root path for a web app whose / is not a 200, or "" to silence a false-red on a non-HTTP preview

name: APP_PREVIEW_PORT_BASE
description: Base of the free-port search range the feature/resolver lanes scan for the app-preview server (they try base..base+98; default: 8501, reproducing today's 8501-8599 block); non-numeric falls back to 8501
when_to_surface: Set to an app's own port convention (e.g. 8080, 3000) so the preview binds where its stack expects; consumed by herd-feature.sh + herd-resolve.sh

name: HEALTHCHECK_CMD
description: Project health command: exit 0 clean, exit 1 code error, exit 2 data/env tolerated. herd init seeds .herd/healthcheck.project.sh from the template matching scout's detected stack (Go/Rust/Java/Node/Python)
when_to_surface: Required for the heavy healthcheck profile; blank falls back to bash -n syntax check only

name: HEALTHCHECK_HEAVY_GLOB
description: Egrep pattern of diff paths that force the heavy profile; blank (default) means every change is heavy
when_to_surface: When only certain changed files need the full test suite — match your own layout (e.g. ^app/, ^cmd/|^internal/, ^src/); leave blank if unsure

name: BASELINE_AWARE_GATE
description: Baseline-aware healthcheck gate (HERD-190): a heavy code error whose failing tests ALL already fail on the base (origin/main) is treated as INHERITED (a tolerated data/env warning), not a merge-blocking code error, so a fix-PR never deadlocks on a base failure it did not introduce. Only ever downgrades a red to a warning; byte-identical when the base is green (empty base known-failure set = every PR failure is introduced) and fully fail-soft (an unresolvable/unparseable base blocks exactly as before): on (default) | off
when_to_surface: Leave on so ungated base failures never deadlock the fix-PRs that clean them up (herdkit enables it); set off to force the classic absolute pass/fail gate. The watcher passes the base checkout as HERD_BASELINE_DIR=$MAIN so no throwaway worktree is created

name: APP_SURFACE_GLOB
description: Egrep pattern of diff paths that constitute the app surface; blank (default) disables the interaction gate with zero behavior change
when_to_surface: Set for projects with an interactive app (e.g. ^app/) so the healthcheck can gate or flag widget→output interaction tests on app-facing PRs

name: INTERACTION_TEST_CMD
description: Command that drives an input and asserts the dependent output changed (exit 0 clean, 1 code error, 2 data/env); framework-neutral — e.g. a UI test harness that sets a value, re-runs, and asserts the output moved
when_to_surface: Set alongside APP_SURFACE_GLOB so an app-surface PR is gated on real interactivity; when APP_SURFACE_GLOB matches but this is blank the healthcheck emits a loud warning that a render smoke cannot see widget→output causality

name: ATTRIBUTION_POLICY
description: Commit-attribution lint policy (HERD-121): '' (default off — lint absent, output byte-identical) | no-ai-coauthor. When no-ai-coauthor, the healthcheck scans the PR's commits (git log <DEFAULT_BRANCH>..HEAD) for AI co-author markers (Co-Authored-By: Claude*, 'Generated with Claude' lines) and fails as a code-error naming the offending sha+line. The 'Never co-author Claude' rule in AGENTS.md is advisory prose without this; set no-ai-coauthor to enforce it deterministically at the gate. The commit-walking helper (_herd_attr_scan in scripts/herd/commit-lint.sh) is reusable: the sibling COMMIT_CONVENTION lint sources it next. Ships dormant: default '' → zero behavior change
when_to_surface: Set to no-ai-coauthor to make AI co-authorship a hard healthcheck gate rather than a silently-violable convention

name: COMMIT_CONVENTION
description: Commit-message convention lint (HERD-124): '' (default off — lint absent, output byte-identical) | an egrep pattern. When set, the healthcheck walks the PR's commits (git log <DEFAULT_BRANCH>..HEAD, via the shared _herd_commit_shas helper in scripts/herd/commit-lint.sh that the HERD-121 attribution scan also rides) and requires every commit SUBJECT to match the pattern; a non-conforming subject fails as a code-error naming the offending sha + subject + pattern. Fail-soft: an INVALID regex WARNS and skips the lint (never a false red). Reuses HERD-121's commit-walking primitive rather than duplicating it. Ships dormant: default '' → zero behavior change. E.g. Conventional Commits: '^(feat|fix|docs|style|refactor|perf|test|build|ci|chore|revert)(\(.+\))?!?: .+'
when_to_surface: Set to an egrep pattern (e.g. a Conventional Commits regex) to make commit-message shape a hard healthcheck gate rather than a silently-violable convention; leave empty (default) for byte-identical legacy behavior

name: SMOKE_CMD
description: Optional smoke test run by herd-resolve.sh after a conflict fix before pushing
when_to_surface: When a quick integration check is faster than the full healthcheck during conflict resolution

name: DENY_PATHS
description: Space-separated paths never committed; scribe and lanes are scoped away from these
when_to_surface: For secrets, credentials, or generated dirs that must never reach git (e.g. .herd/secrets)

name: REVIEW_CHECKLIST
description: Repo-relative path to a project risk checklist injected into the pre-merge review gate
when_to_surface: When the review gate should verify project-specific invariants beyond generic correctness

name: COORDINATOR_CMD
description: Slash command name for the rendered coordinator skill (default: /coordinator)
when_to_surface: When the project uses a non-default coordinator command name

name: HERD_DRIVER
description: Driver binding for the coordinator skill's runtime-specific control surface (multiplexer + agent runtime): selects templates/drivers/<name>.driver, whose DRIVER_* incantations are substituted into the coordinator skill at render time via the same token machinery as the other config-driven values. Four production drivers ship — herdr-claude (herdr multiplexer + Claude Code runtime; the default, which renders the skill functionally identical to before the abstraction), headless (no herdr panes — agents run detached and the watcher/journal/notifications run headless, for Windows/CI/headless Linux), codex (herdr mux + OpenAI Codex CLI runtime — the DRIVER_AGENT_* exec surface rebound to `codex`), and grok (herdr mux + xAI Grok Build CLI runtime — rebound to `grok`) — plus stub, a non-Claude PROOF/REFERENCE driver (HERD-177) proving the portability seam runs a non-Claude runtime end-to-end (not for production; use codex/grok). An unknown value fails the render loudly, listing the available drivers
when_to_surface: When adopting a non-herdr/non-Claude runtime for the coordinator control surface (list/focus/send-to agents); leave unset or herdr-claude for today's exact behavior

name: HERD_VERSION
description: Engine contract version pinned in .herd/config; herd upgrade reads and bumps this value
when_to_surface: Internal version pin; a mismatch triggers a warning on upgrade

name: HERD_REPO
description: GitHub repo where engine bugs escalate via herd report (e.g. owner/herdkit)
when_to_surface: Required for github-backed herd report: unset ⇒ it REFUSES rather than defaulting to a stranger's repo (only the herdkit engine repo self-targets)

name: HERD_BRAND
description: Display brand shown in user-facing doctor/preflight diagnostics (herd-preflight.sh); a consumer who has branded their own workflow sees THEIR name instead of the literal WORKSPACE_NAME. Defaults through WORKSPACE_NAME (then 'herdkit'), so unset output is byte-unchanged.
when_to_surface: Set when the display brand should differ from the workspace name.

name: WATCHER_AUTOMERGE
description: Legacy boolean lever (true/false); superseded by MERGE_POLICY when set
when_to_surface: Prefer MERGE_POLICY; kept for backwards compatibility with pre-v1 configs

name: MERGE_POLICY
description: Merge gate policy: auto (watcher merges on pass) | approve (coordinator approval required) | observe (watcher never merges)
when_to_surface: Primary merge control lever; when approve, coordinator uses herd-approve.sh to sign off PRs

name: HUMAN_VERIFY_POLICY
description: How the watcher treats a PR that declares a HUMAN-VERIFY: block under MERGE_POLICY=auto: hold (default — today's EXACT behavior: sha-keyed approve-style hold released by herd-approve.sh approve) | coordinator (keep the hold but notify loudly and flag it coordinator-actionable, so a coordinator/agent runs the declared steps then approves) | auto (treat the declared steps as informational only — journal + PR-comment them and merge on green gates). Unknown/empty falls back to hold. Honored by agent-watch.sh, surfaced by herd-approve.sh
when_to_surface: Set to coordinator for an auto pipeline where a coordinator/agent executes human-verify steps and signs off, or auto to let a PR's declared steps merge as informational; leave unset/hold (default) to keep every HUMAN-VERIFY PR held for explicit sign-off. Opt-in only, never self-activates; watcher-affecting, so it takes effect on the next watcher restart

name: MERGE_METHOD
description: git merge strategy used by the watcher: merge | squash | rebase
when_to_surface: Controls how PRs land; merge preserves history, squash condenses, rebase linearizes

name: PR_FLOW
description: How builders open PRs: direct (open ready to merge — default, today's behavior) | draft (builder runs 'gh pr create --draft'; the watcher already holds DRAFT PRs until they are marked ready). Threaded into the LANE RULES text only (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh); an unknown value falls back to direct
when_to_surface: Set to draft to keep new PRs out of the auto-merge path until someone promotes them to ready; leave direct for today's open-ready flow

name: PR_READY_WHEN
description: Who promotes a DRAFT PR to ready-for-review: builder (self, once the healthcheck passes — default) | coordinator | human. Only consulted when PR_FLOW=draft; threaded into the LANE RULES wording only; an unknown value falls back to builder
when_to_surface: Set alongside PR_FLOW=draft to decide who moves a draft to ready (the builder itself, the coordinator, or a human); ignored when PR_FLOW=direct

name: PUSH_GATE
description: Gate-then-upload: hold a FINISHED builder for human review BEFORE anything reaches GitHub. '' (default, off) | human. With human, the builder lane completes work + healthcheck but STOPS before git push / gh pr create, recording a sha-keyed awaiting-push hold (push-gate.sh) the watcher shows as 'ready · awaiting push approval' with the worktree path; herd-approve.sh approve <slug> resumes push + PR creation. A new commit invalidates a prior approval (sha-keyed). Unknown value falls back to off. Complements PR_FLOW=draft (gates AFTER upload) and MERGE_POLICY=approve (gates AFTER review). Threaded into the LANE RULES (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh) and consumed by herd-approve.sh + agent-watch.sh
when_to_surface: Set to human to require human sign-off on the LOCAL diff before a builder pushes/opens a PR; leave unset/'' for today's open-on-finish flow. Watcher-affecting (console row) so it takes effect on the next watcher restart; opt-in, never self-activates

name: DELETE_BRANCH_ON_MERGE
description: Whether the merged head branch is auto-deleted: true | false (default false — branches are retained, today's behavior). When true the watcher passes --delete-branch to `gh pr merge` on the MERGE_METHOD path (composing with the HERD-156 --match-head-commit sha pin), so a merged feature branch is cleaned up instead of accumulating on the remote.
when_to_surface: Set true to have merged feature branches deleted automatically instead of accumulating.

name: LOCAL_REVIEW
description: Pre-PR local review mode: none (default) | pre-pr | risk-scoped. pre-pr makes every builder pass a local adversarial correctness review (herd-review.sh --local) before opening its PR. risk-scoped runs that review ONLY when the builder's diff surface (git diff DEFAULT_BRANCH...HEAD --name-only) matches LOCAL_REVIEW_GLOB, so low-risk diffs skip straight to the PR; the watcher's post-PR review gate is unchanged and remains authoritative either way. Consumed by herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh (threaded into the builder task spec)
when_to_surface: Set pre-pr to review every diff locally before its PR, or risk-scoped (with LOCAL_REVIEW_GLOB) to review only engine-risk diffs and skip low-risk ones; leave none to rely solely on the post-PR gate

name: LOCAL_REVIEW_GLOB
description: Egrep pattern of changed-file paths (git diff DEFAULT_BRANCH...HEAD --name-only) that make a LOCAL_REVIEW=risk-scoped builder run the pre-PR local review; a diff touching no matching path skips it and goes straight to the PR. Same egrep semantics as REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB / HEALTHCHECK_HEAVY_GLOB, but a DEDICATED key so the pre-PR risk surface is scoped independently of the post-PR review tiering. Blank (default) → dormant; under risk-scoped an empty or invalid glob fails SOFT (loud warn) to unconditional pre-pr, never to a silent skip. Ignored under LOCAL_REVIEW none/pre-pr
when_to_surface: Set (e.g. '^scripts/herd/|^bin/') alongside LOCAL_REVIEW=risk-scoped to spend pre-PR review quota only on engine-risk diffs; leave blank if not using risk-scoped review

name: CONTEXT_PROVISION
description: Builder context-provisioning surface: a SPACE-SEPARATED list of grounding sources injected into the STABLE region of every spawned builder's task-spec preamble (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh, via herd_context_provision_preamble in herd-config.sh), so builders start GROUNDED instead of re-exploring the repo each session. Empty/unset (default) → NOTHING injected, task specs byte-identical to today. Injected grounding lives entirely in the prompt-cache-shared STABLE prefix (before the per-item Refs trailer), never interleaved with per-task text. Supported sources: codemap → a pointer telling the builder to read the deterministic code map committed at docs/codemap.md (produced by 'herd codemap' — the herdkit engine tree in the engine repo, or the consuming project's OWN language-aware source tree elsewhere, auto-detected) FIRST to orient; symbol-index → a pointer to the function-level def→caller index committed at docs/symbol-index.md (produced by 'herd symbol-index') for jumping to a function's def or its cross-file callers, with its honest-scope caveat that it is a heuristic token scan (same-name defs and dynamic dispatch are ambiguous), not ground truth. Extensible: future sources (project context notes, MCP tool hints) plug in as new tokens; an UNKNOWN token is ignored (forward-compatible), never an error
when_to_surface: Set to 'codemap' to inject a pointer to the committed docs/codemap.md into each builder's task spec so it loads the repo map instead of re-exploring the tree, and/or 'symbol-index' for the committed docs/symbol-index.md function-level def→caller index; leave empty (default) for today's byte-identical task specs. Space-separate multiple sources (e.g. 'codemap symbol-index') as more grounding lanes ship

name: WATCHER_VIEW
description: Watcher lens — narrows WHICH open PRs the console displays/considers each tick: all (default; unset behaves identically to today — every open PR) | mine (only PRs authored by WATCHER_VIEW_AUTHOR, or the resolved gh user) | review-queue (only PRs whose reviewDecision is REVIEW_REQUIRED, i.e. awaiting review) | deps (only PRs carrying the WATCHER_VIEW_DEPS_LABEL label). SELECTION only: it never relaxes a merge gate and can never blind-merge a hidden PR; an unknown value falls back to all with a loud warning
when_to_surface: Set to focus a shared repo's watcher on a subset (your own PRs, the review backlog, or dependency PRs); leave unset/all to watch everything exactly as before

name: WATCHER_VIEW_AUTHOR
description: Filter/identity: only show PRs authored by this GitHub login; also supplies the identity for WATCHER_VIEW=mine (when blank, mine resolves the login via `gh api user`)
when_to_surface: Set to scope the watcher to one author, or to pin the identity for the `mine` lens without a gh lookup

name: WATCHER_VIEW_ASSIGNEE
description: Filter: only show PRs assigned to this GitHub login (ANDs with the lens and other filters)
when_to_surface: Set to watch only PRs assigned to a specific teammate

name: WATCHER_VIEW_LABEL
description: Filter: only show PRs carrying this label (ANDs with the lens and other filters)
when_to_surface: Set to scope the watcher to a single label (e.g. a release train or area label)

name: WATCHER_VIEW_STATUS
description: Filter: only show PRs whose mergeStateStatus equals this value, e.g. CLEAN | BLOCKED | BEHIND | UNSTABLE (ANDs with the lens and other filters)
when_to_surface: Set to focus on PRs in a specific gate state (e.g. only CLEAN, or only BLOCKED PRs needing attention)

name: WATCHER_VIEW_DEPS_LABEL
description: The label the `deps` lens matches on (default: dependencies)
when_to_surface: Override when dependency PRs in your repo use a different label

name: WATCHER_SCOPE
description: Team-mode auto-merge ownership gate: mine (default) | all. Narrows WHICH open PRs the watcher may AUTO-MERGE, layered on top of the WATCHER_VIEW lens; a NARROWING gate — it can only ever WITHHOLD a merge, never authorize one the gates (healthcheck + review + re-verify) would deny. mine = today's exact SOLO behavior (auto-merge the operator's own PRs; the ownership probe stays dormant, byte-identical to before). all = team mode: teammates' PRs are DISPLAYED but auto-merge is STRICTLY scoped to PRs owned by WATCHER_OWNER — a teammate's PR shows "not mine — manual" and is NEVER auto-merged even when MERGEABLE+CLEAN+approved. Unknown value falls back to the safe default mine
when_to_surface: Set to all on a SHARED repo so the watcher displays teammates' PRs but only auto-merges your own; leave unset/mine for a solo install (behavior unchanged). Fail-closed: in team mode an unresolvable owner WITHHOLDS auto-merge

name: WATCHER_OWNER
description: Operator identity (GitHub login) that OWNS auto-merge in team mode (WATCHER_SCOPE=all). Resolution order: WATCHER_OWNER (explicit) → WATCHER_VIEW_AUTHOR (reuse the lens identity) → `gh api user`; resolved at most once per process. When WATCHER_SCOPE=all and this cannot be resolved, auto-merge is WITHHELD for safety (fail-closed — a teammate's PR is never blind-merged on unconfirmed ownership). Ignored under the default WATCHER_SCOPE=mine
when_to_surface: Set alongside WATCHER_SCOPE=all to pin the login whose PRs auto-merge without a gh lookup; unnecessary in the default solo scope

name: REVIEW_CONCURRENCY
description: Maximum pre-merge reviews the watcher dispatches in parallel (default: 2)
when_to_surface: Raise for review throughput at higher model cost; lower to 1 to serialize reviews

name: SPAWN_AHEAD
description: Advisory spawn-rate lead over the review gate (default: 1). Governs two mechanisms: (1) the lane advisory gate (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh) HOLDS a new builder spawn when the review pipeline is saturated (live+queued reviews ≥ REVIEW_CONCURRENCY) AND in-flight builders already exceed REVIEW_CONCURRENCY + SPAWN_AHEAD; (2) the watcher's durable spawn-queue drain (spawn.sh / spawn-step.sh) pops at most REVIEW_CONCURRENCY + SPAWN_AHEAD - active_builders intents per tick, so the queue never feeds faster than the review gate can process. Advisory only: --force / HERD_FORCE_SPAWN=1 bypasses the lane gate for urgent items
when_to_surface: Keep at 1 to keep one build ahead of the gate so the pipeline stays fed; set 0 for strict no-surplus (never build past REVIEW_CONCURRENCY in-flight while the gate is full); raise to allow a deeper builder lead when review is fast relative to build time

name: HEALTH_CONCURRENCY
description: Maximum healthcheck suites the watcher runs at once (default: 1 = serialize); all feature worktrees share one git object store, so overlapping suites race on shared .git locks and can paint a false-red for a clean PR — the watcher queues a PR ("health-check · queued") until a slot frees
when_to_surface: Keep at 1 unless the project's healthcheck is fully worktree-isolated (no shared object store); raise only if suites provably do not contend

name: REVIEW_INFLIGHT_TIMEOUT
description: Restart-safe review-dispatch timeout in seconds (HERD-185). A pre-merge reviewer runs in the BACKGROUND holding a REVIEW_CONCURRENCY slot via an on-disk inflight marker that records pid + pid-start-time + dispatch timestamp. The every-tick corpse sweep frees that slot the moment the reviewer is a corpse (pid dead/recycled) OR its age — computed FROM THE MARKER'S OWN timestamp, so ANY watcher instance including one that restarted mid-run can enforce it — exceeds this many seconds, in which case the live reviewer is SIGTERMed, an infra_event review_timeout is journaled, and the retry budget is counted. Set well above any legitimate review (Opus ~7 min) so a healthy reviewer is never killed. Non-numeric -> the built-in default. Default 1800 (30 min)
when_to_surface: Lower only if a hung reviewer must be reclaimed faster (at more risk of killing a legitimately slow review); raise for very slow reviewers. Leave at 1800 in almost all cases

name: HEALTH_INFLIGHT_TIMEOUT
description: Restart-safe health-dispatch timeout in seconds (HERD-185). The healthcheck suite now runs ASYNCHRONOUSLY in a background worker holding a HEALTH_CONCURRENCY slot via the same restart-safe inflight marker as the review gate. The every-tick corpse sweep frees that slot when the worker is a corpse (pid dead/recycled) OR its marker age exceeds this many seconds, in which case the live suite is SIGTERMed, an infra_event health_timeout is journaled, and the slot is freed for a re-dispatch. A holder pid equal to the watcher itself is never SIGTERMed. Set well above the heaviest suite (main-health runs the full heavy suite). Non-numeric -> the built-in default. Default 1800 (30 min)
when_to_surface: Lower only if a hung suite must be reclaimed faster (at more risk of killing a legitimately slow suite); raise for very slow suites. Leave at 1800 in almost all cases

name: GATE_DISPATCH
description: When the watcher's action pass fires the pre-merge review relative to the healthcheck for a (pr,sha): serial (default) | parallel. serial → today's EXACT behavior, byte-identical: the review dispatches only AFTER the healthcheck outcome lands, so gate wall-clock = health + review. parallel → the review dispatches at the same action-pass tick the healthcheck STARTS, so the two gates overlap and wall-clock is roughly max(health, review). The MERGE decision is UNCHANGED either way — a merge still requires BOTH gates green (no ordering shortcut on the decision itself); REVIEW_CONCURRENCY and the sha-keyed review-once semantics are respected unchanged, and a health CODE-ERROR while a review is in flight does NOT kill the reviewer (it finishes and its verdict is recorded — the sha is blocked by health anyway). Unknown value → serial (fail safe)
when_to_surface: Set to parallel to cut gate wall-clock by overlapping the review with the healthcheck. Tradeoff: a sha whose healthcheck fails wastes ONE review run (the review already dispatched) — cheap under REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB tiering, which sends low-risk diffs to the cheap reviewer or skips them. Leave unset/serial (default) to keep behavior byte-identical; opt-in only, watcher-affecting so it takes effect on the next watcher restart

name: GATE_STATUS
description: Whether the watcher posts a `herd/gates` COMMIT STATUS as it clears each (pr,sha): on (default) | off. on → on both-gates-green (healthcheck + adversarial review) the watcher posts state=success — EXACTLY ONCE per (pr,sha) via a sha-keyed ledger ($GATE_STATUS_STATE), best-effort + fail-soft (a failed API write re-tries next tick since the blessing MUST land). It posts ONLY success: it NEVER posts a non-passing pending/failure status, because a non-passing commit status flips a CLEAN sha to mergeStateStatus=UNSTABLE (neither CLEAN nor BLOCKED) in the default unprotected config, stranding the PR out of the merge loop and silently breaking the block/override/auto-refix paths. A gate FAIL posts NOTHING — the fail-safe rests entirely on the ABSENCE of success. The blessing is posted ONLY by a watcher that actually RAN the gates, so pairing it with `require herd/gates` GitHub branch protection (recipe: docs/governance-gates.md) makes the gate FAIL-SAFE across seats and collaborators who keep merge rights: anyone may merge, but nothing UNGATED can — a commit no watcher blessed has no success status and is unmergeable under protection. Under protection a fresh PR reports BLOCKED (missing required check); the watcher gates such a PR specially (runs gates, posts the blessing, but still merges only when CLEAN) so requiring herd/gates never deadlocks. --match-head-commit in do_merge stays as the belt. In team mode (WATCHER_SCOPE=all) the watcher checks the sha's existing status BEFORE dispatching gates and does not re-gate a sha another seat already blessed (cross-seat dedup, ledger-healed so a ledger loss never strands an owned PR). off → byte-inert: no status posted, no read. Unknown value → on. Consumed by agent-watch.sh
when_to_surface: Leave on (default) so branch protection can require herd/gates and merges stay gated even for collaborators with merge rights; apply the one-time branch-protection recipe in docs/governance-gates.md. Set to off only where the watcher must never touch commit statuses (e.g. a repo with no branch protection where the extra check row is unwanted). Watcher-affecting (next watcher restart)

name: NATIVE_BURST
description: Master switch for the native-burst bounded read-only FAN-OUT seam (scripts/herd/burst.sh): off (default) | on. OFF → today's EXACT serial behavior, byte-identical — the research drainer's Explore fan-out is un-hinted and the pre-merge review runs as a single reviewer. ON → read-only work may BURST (fan out several CONCURRENT calls bounded by REVIEW_CONCURRENCY, the ceiling): the research lane (research.sh) hints the drainer a bounded concurrent-Explore width, and the review gate (herd-review.sh) may run a bounded REVIEW PANEL (see REVIEW_PANEL). WRITE lanes (scribe/backlog/merge) NEVER burst — they stay strictly serial. Unknown/blank → off (fail safe)
when_to_surface: Set to on to cut wall-clock on read-only research + review by fanning out concurrent calls bounded by REVIEW_CONCURRENCY; leave unset/off (default) to keep behavior byte-identical. Additive + opt-in

name: REVIEW_PANEL
description: How many CONCURRENT read-only reviewer passes the pre-merge review runs over the SAME diff when NATIVE_BURST=on — a bounded 'review panel' (more independent eyes catch more correctness bugs). Effective panel size is min(REVIEW_PANEL, REVIEW_CONCURRENCY). Default 1 → a single reviewer, i.e. today's byte-identical behavior; the panel only engages at >1 AND with NATIVE_BURST=on, on the HEADLESS reviewer path. Verdict combination is FAIL-SAFE: ANY member's BLOCK blocks the merge, a merge needs >=1 PASS and zero BLOCKs, and a total-death of all members is an INFRA-FAIL retry (never a cached BLOCK)
when_to_surface: Raise (with NATIVE_BURST=on) to run 2-3 independent reviewer passes per diff for higher bug recall at proportional model cost; keep at 1 for a single reviewer

name: INFRA_BREAKER_MAX
description: INFRA-timeout circuit breaker threshold (HERD-110): after this many CONSECUTIVE INFRA failures — non-verdict reviewer deaths (a claude exec-hang / env failure / a dispatched reviewer that died WITHOUT writing a verdict), tracked GLOBALLY across all PRs — the watcher OPENS a circuit breaker and STOPS dispatching new pre-merge reviews/healthchecks into what looks like a dead/hung environment, surfacing a loud 'infra circuit open' status row + journal event. After INFRA_BREAKER_COOLDOWN seconds the breaker goes HALF-OPEN for a SINGLE probe: a real verdict CLOSEs it (env recovered), another non-verdict death RE-OPENs it. CRITICAL: a real code BLOCK or PASS verdict proves the env is alive and RESETS the counter — it NEVER trips the breaker; only non-verdict deaths count. 0 (default) = OFF, byte-inert
when_to_surface: Turn on (a small N like 3-5) for unattended/overnight runs so a dead reviewer environment (exec-hang, transient infra outage) is detected after N consecutive non-verdict deaths and the watcher stops burning cycles re-dispatching into it, instead of hammering every PR forever; leave 0 (default) to keep behavior byte-identical

name: INFRA_BREAKER_COOLDOWN
description: Seconds the INFRA circuit breaker (HERD-110, INFRA_BREAKER_MAX) stays OPEN before it admits a SINGLE half-open probe retry. While open, new review/health dispatch is suppressed; once the cooldown elapses the next candidate becomes the lone probe whose outcome closes (real verdict) or re-opens (another death) the breaker. Non-numeric → 300. Only consulted when INFRA_BREAKER_MAX >= 1
