when_to_surface: Tune the recovery cadence: shorter → probe a possibly-recovered environment sooner (more retries into a still-dead env); longer → wait out a longer outage before re-probing. Default 300s (5 min)

name: WATCH_CLAUDE_PROBE_TIMEOUT
description: Claude exec-hang probe timeout in seconds (HERD-108): on some environments `claude` WEDGES on invocation — every exec hangs before the process finishes starting (e.g. the macOS com.apple.quarantine _dyld_start hang) — so every review/refix dispatch spawns a corpse and the watcher's poll loop burns cycles against a hang it cannot see. When armed (N>=1), the watcher probes `claude --version` under a HARD N-second timeout ONCE per tick BEFORE dispatching review/refix for its merge candidates; a timeout HOLDS that tick's review/refix (a cached-PASS PR still merges) with a loud 'claude exec-hang' status row + a journal infra_event, journaled once per hang episode. Complements the HERD-110 INFRA_BREAKER (which only reacts AFTER reviewers die without a verdict) by catching the wedge up front; `herd doctor`'s own `claude responds` check reports the same hang at diagnosis time. Only a genuine TIMEOUT counts as a hang — a broken (non-zero exit) or absent claude is fail-soft and never holds the queue. 0 (default) = OFF, byte-inert (no probe exec, no journal)
when_to_surface: Arm (a small N like 5) for unattended/overnight runs so a wedged claude is DETECTED and surfaced instead of silently stalling every review/refix; leave 0 (default) to keep behavior byte-identical

name: REVIEW_AUTOFIX
description: Auto-bounce BLOCK reviews to the builder agent: true | false (default false); when true, the watcher delivers a re-task prompt and re-tasks the idle builder on each new BLOCK sha, up to REFIX_MAX_ROUNDS
when_to_surface: Enable to remove the human re-task step after a BLOCK review; disable (default) for projects that prefer explicit coordinator approval before re-work

name: REFIX_MAX_ROUNDS
description: Maximum auto-refix rounds per PR before escalating to "needs you" (default: 3); counts across all shas for a PR
when_to_surface: Raise for longer fix cycles; lower to 1 for a single bounce with immediate escalation; requires REVIEW_AUTOFIX=true

name: HEALTHCHECK_AUTOFIX
description: Auto-bounce a reproduced pre-merge healthcheck CODE ERROR to the builder agent, exactly as REVIEW_AUTOFIX bounces a BLOCK review: true | false (default false); the re-task prompt carries the failing test line + the tailable suite log path, is delivered once per (pr,sha), and SHARES one per-PR round budget with the review refix (REFIX_MAX_ROUNDS counts review + health bounces together). Limit-parked and dead/missing agents are preflighted (never typed at); a tab-leak-guard trip is infra and never bounced; exhausting the budget escalates to a needs-you row carrying the blocker and the remedy
when_to_surface: Enable to close the loop on a red healthcheck without a human re-task step; disable (default) when a code-error red should wait for explicit coordinator triage

name: CODEMAP_AUTOREFRESH
description: After a PR merges to the default branch, the watcher regenerates the committed code maps — docs/codemap.md (file-level; the engine tree in the engine repo, or the consuming project's own language-aware source tree elsewhere — codemap.sh auto-detects the mode) AND docs/symbol-index.md (function-level def→caller index) — via their deterministic, LLM-free native scans and, only when a map's content actually changed, commits it STRAIGHT to the default branch (no PR, mirroring the BACKLOG.md convention; messages "chore: refresh codemap after PR #N" / "chore: refresh symbol-index after PR #N") and pushes ff-safe: on (default) | off. Fully fail-soft — a map problem never blocks or fails the merge. off → byte-inert: the watcher never touches either map
when_to_surface: Leave on (default) so the committed engine maps stay fresh for coordinator/builder context at zero token cost; set off to freeze them and refresh only manually via herd codemap / herd symbol-index. Independent of the read-only staleness probes (herd codemap --check / herd symbol-index --check / the herd status CODEMAP row), which always work regardless of this lever

name: MAIN_HEALTH_TICK
description: After a PR merges to the default branch, the watcher runs the healthcheck suite against the freshly ff'd default-branch HEAD to catch a RED main AT MERGE TIME — the failure mode where two independently-green PRs merge into a broken COMBINATION (e.g. the #226 advise exit-code collision that left main red ~2h, found only when later unrelated PRs inherited it). sha-keyed so each main sha runs ONCE; runs the FULL (heavy) suite — a 'light' check against the default-branch checkout is a zero-file vacuous green that could silently clear a real red; honors HEALTH_CONCURRENCY. On a reproduced red it raises a LOUD persistent 'MAIN RED — <failing test> (since #N)' console row + a notification, cleared when a later sha goes green: on | off (default). An ALARM only — never a merge gate, revert, or re-merge; fully fail-soft (a suite that cannot run journals an infra_event, never a false-red). off → byte-inert: no suite, no journal, no row
when_to_surface: Turn on in a repo that wants a merge-time tripwire on the default branch's health (herdkit enables it for itself). Leave off (default) elsewhere: zero behavior change, and the pre-merge health gate still proves every PR green in isolation. Distinct from that gate — this catches only the broken-combination class the per-PR gate structurally cannot see

name: WATCHER_FLAIR
description: Watcher status-console FLAIR pack (HERD-147): on | off (default). ADDITIVE cosmetic layer on the live 'herd watch' console — (a) a merge CELEBRATION: the status tick after a merge prints one theme-colored line '🐑 #<pr> joins the flock · <n> grazing'; (b) a PASTURE HEADER: one line rendering the in-flight herd by state — 🐑 grazing (building), 💤 idle, ✅ in the pen (done). Colors come ONLY from the active theme's C_* vars (theme.sh); NO_COLOR / non-tty renders plain. HARD RULE: flair NEVER softens a red/dead/needs-you state — a 💀 dead or ⚠️ needs-you builder is exactly as loud as today, and OFF (default) is byte-inert: every console byte is identical to before this feature
when_to_surface: Turn on for a cosmetic morale layer on the watcher console (herdkit enables it for itself after this ships); leave off (default) for a byte-identical console. Watcher-affecting, so it takes effect on the next watcher restart; opt-in, never self-activates and never changes a gate or a merge decision

name: OPERATOR_INBOX
description: Cross-seat OPERATOR INBOX (HERD-184): so coordination messages left as comments reach an autonomous coordinator automatically. off (default) | on. OFF -> byte-inert: no inbox reader runs, no fetch, no console section, output identical to today. ON -> each watcher tick (throttled on an interval, not every 4s repaint) surfaces NEW comments by OTHER authors as a 'operator inbox' console section (needs-you-adjacent, above the in-flight rows) plus one notify-once per comment id. TWO feeds, one surface: (1) PR-COMMENT feed -- new comments by non-self authors on the open PRs this seat authored or is gating (the tick's already-fetched PR set), read with a dedicated gated `gh pr view --json comments` (the core tick's --json field set is UNCHANGED); (2) TRACKER feed -- new comments by other operators on items this seat claimed/marked, read via the active SCRIBE_BACKEND's OPTIONAL _backend_list_inbox_comments op (implemented for linear; absent on file/github/jira -> that feed is simply empty). Comment ids are cached in a seen-ledger so each surfaces/notifies exactly ONCE; the section renders the most-recent entries newest-first. ADDITIVE + FAIL-SOFT by contract: a missing reader, an api error, or absent credentials yields an EMPTY inbox, NEVER a red row; scoped strictly to display/notify and never touches the gate loop or a merge decision. Opt-in, never self-activates; watcher-affecting, so it takes effect on the next watcher restart
when_to_surface: Set to on for an autonomous/unattended coordinator so cross-seat requests left as PR or tracker comments (e.g. a 'don't self-merge, main broke' note) are surfaced automatically instead of being silently missed; leave off (default) for a byte-identical console. Pairs with the tracker backend (linear) for the tracker feed

name: BUDGET_DAILY
description: Daily spend CEILING in USD (HERD-95): ENFORCE a budget, don't just MEASURE it. `herd cost` already prices every builder/review/agent session and journals a `cost` event at merge; this key turns that ledger into a rail. EMPTY (default) = DORMANT: byte-identical to no budget. Set it to a USD number and, when TODAY's (UTC) recorded spend — summed from the journal's `cost` events by cost.sh cost_day_total, so NO cost math is reimplemented — EXCEEDS it, TWO enforcement points engage: (1) the watcher PAUSES draining the durable spawn queue (agent-watch.sh _drain_spawn_queue), journaling budget_drain_paused once per over-budget stretch and budget_drain_resumed when spend falls back under; (2) each builder lane (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh) REFUSES a new spawn with one loud 🛑 line BEFORE any worktree/tab is created, journaling budget_spawn_refused. OVERRIDABLE: HERD_FORCE_SPAWN=1 (or a lane's --force) spawns anyway and journals budget_spawn_forced. FAIL-SOFT: a missing journal / no python3 / a non-numeric value never blocks a spawn. Consumed by agent-watch.sh + the lanes via cost.sh.
when_to_surface: Set to a USD ceiling (e.g. 25) for an unattended/autonomous run so a runaway day stops spawning new builders automatically instead of only surfacing when a human reads `herd cost`; leave empty (default) for no ceiling. This is the ENFORCEMENT half of the cost program — `herd cost` measures, BUDGET_DAILY enforces. Watcher-affecting (drain pause) on the next watcher restart; the lanes read it live per spawn.

name: MODEL_ESCALATE_GLOB
description: Egrep -i pattern of task text that forces the MODEL_FEATURE tier in either lane, overriding MODEL_QUICK and any per-spawn HERD_QUICK_MODEL/HERD_FEATURE_MODEL override (deterministic model step-up, analogous to HEALTHCHECK_HEAVY_GLOB); blank (default) → off
when_to_surface: Set when judgment-heavy engine surfaces keep getting routed through the cheap quick lane and misbuilding, so those tasks deterministically get the feature-tier model without a per-spawn override

name: REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB
description: Egrep pattern of changed-file paths that opts into RISK-TIERED pre-merge review (analogous to HEALTHCHECK_HEAVY_GLOB / MODEL_ESCALATE_GLOB); blank (default) → off, every PR gets the full $MODEL_REVIEW (Opus) review UNCHANGED. When set: paths matching it (engine surface) or a diff over REVIEW_ESCALATE_MAXFILES files → STRONG tier ($MODEL_REVIEW); a docs/test-only diff (only *.md and tests/) → review SKIPPED with a source=skipped-low-risk PASS recorded (no reviewer spawned, still sha-cached); any other small low-risk diff → CHEAP tier ($REVIEW_MODEL_CHEAP). Classification fails SAFE (unreadable/empty diff → STRONG)
when_to_surface: Set to reserve the expensive Opus review for engine-critical paths and large diffs, cutting the single biggest recurring engine cost. Tradeoff: a cheaper reviewer can miss subtle bugs and glob/size classification can misjudge a risky diff, so scope the glob to genuinely engine-critical paths

name: REVIEW_MODEL_CHEAP
description: Cheaper reviewer model tier used for small, low-risk diffs when REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB tiering is active (default: claude-sonnet-4-6); ignored when REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB is blank
when_to_surface: Override to pick the low-risk reviewer tier (e.g. a different sonnet/haiku model); only takes effect once REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB opts into tiered review

name: REVIEW_ESCALATE_MAXFILES
description: Under REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB tiering, a diff touching MORE than this many files escalates to the STRONG tier even without a glob match (default: 10; non-numeric → 10); ignored when REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB is blank
when_to_surface: Lower to send more diffs to the full review, raise to let larger low-risk diffs use the cheap tier

name: DOCS_ONLY_GLOB
description: Egrep pattern (HERD-89) that opts a PR's review into the cheapest DOCS tier when EVERY changed path matches it; blank (default) → off, byte-identical. Activates the risk-tiered review on its own (REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB need not be set): a diff where every path matches routes to $REVIEW_MODEL_DOCS (a real, cheap adversarial review — NOT the *.md/tests SKIP). ESCALATION WINS: a docs diff also matching REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB, or over REVIEW_ESCALATE_MAXFILES files, is STRONG regardless. Classification fails SAFE (unreadable/empty diff → STRONG)
when_to_surface: Set (e.g. '\.(md|txt)$') to review pure-docs diffs on the cheapest model instead of the full/cheap tier; pin engine-dir docs (e.g. templates/) into REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB so they escalate rather than downgrade

name: REVIEW_MODEL_DOCS
description: Cheapest reviewer model tier used for pure-docs diffs when DOCS_ONLY_GLOB matches every changed path (default: claude-haiku-4-5); ignored when DOCS_ONLY_GLOB is blank
when_to_surface: Override to pick the docs-tier reviewer model; only takes effect once DOCS_ONLY_GLOB opts a diff into the docs tier

name: REVIEW_MODEL_ESCALATED
description: Reviewer model tier the pre-merge gate ESCALATES to once a PR has burned enough failed refix rounds (agent-watch.sh): the next review is forced up to this strong tier regardless of the REVIEW_ESCALATE_GLOB size/path classification. Default claude-opus-4-8.
when_to_surface: Override to pin the repeated-failure escalation tier; leave default for Opus-on-struggling-PR.

name: REVIEW_EVIDENCE_ESCALATE_ROUNDS
description: How many failed refix rounds a PR must accumulate before a cheap-tier reviewer PASS is distrusted and the next review is forced up to REVIEW_MODEL_ESCALATED (agent-watch.sh). Default 2; non-numeric → 2.
when_to_surface: Lower to escalate sooner on a struggling PR; raise to give the cheap tier more rounds first.

name: REVIEW_LOG_KEEP
description: How many pre-merge review logs to retain per PR in the rolling review-log tracker (herd-review.sh); older logs fall off. Default 5; non-numeric → 5.
when_to_surface: Raise to keep more review history per PR; lower to trim disk use.

name: DEP_POLL_MIN
description: Initial dep-watcher poll interval in seconds; also the value a state change resets the capped-exponential backoff to (default: 30)
when_to_surface: Lower for snappier dep pickup at more polling cost, raise to poll less often; consumed by dep-watcher.sh

name: DEP_POLL_MAX
description: Maximum dep-watcher poll interval in seconds after backoff widens it while open deps sit unchanged tick-over-tick (default: 300)
when_to_surface: Raise to let long-pending deps be polled even less aggressively over time, lower to keep a floor on responsiveness; consumed by dep-watcher.sh

name: DEP_STALE_TTL
description: Seconds a still-open dep may go without movement before it surfaces as `stalled` (loudly, but never a freeze); dep-watcher applies it to blocked-on deps, agent-watch applies it to a HELD spawn intent (after=<dep>, HERD-94); 0 disables stall surfacing (default: 86400)
when_to_surface: Lower to be warned about a stalled upstream / held spawn sooner, raise to tolerate longer-pending deps, 0 to turn stall surfacing off entirely

name: .herd/links
description: Cross-repo link registry: name|owner/repo|backend|target rows; enables herd report --to and herd link list
when_to_surface: When filing issues against peer repos or routing engine bugs to specific trackers across services

name: .herd/deps
description: Cross-repo dependency list polled by dep-watcher.sh; each row marks a feature as blocked-on an upstream PR
when_to_surface: When a feature waits on a peer-repo PR to merge first; dep-watcher transitions state when upstream merges

name: .herd/secrets
description: API-backend credentials (e.g. LINEAR_API_KEY; jira: JIRA_BASE_URL/JIRA_EMAIL/JIRA_API_TOKEN [+optional JIRA_PROJECT_KEY, JIRA_ISSUE_TYPE]); gitignored and sourced by scribe-step and cmd_backlog
when_to_surface: Required for linear, jira, or other API backends; never put credentials in the committed .herd/config

name: HUMAN-VERIFY:
description: PR-body marker a builder emits to declare manual steps it could not run itself (one step per line); the watcher parses it and holds that PR for sha-keyed approval instead of auto-merging, a new commit re-holds
when_to_surface: When a change has a manual/live verification step (smoke test, UI or pane check, running-app dependency) that the automated gates cannot exercise before merge

name: herd-watch-<workspace>
description: Per-workspace argv0 the watcher (agent-watch.sh) re-execs itself under at startup, so a running watcher is attributable to exactly one workspace in ps/pgrep; herd reload/stop reap only THIS project's watchers (lockfile PID union argv0 match) and never a sibling workspace's, even though both run the identical engine script
when_to_surface: When attributing or reaping a watcher across parallel projects: match pgrep -f "herd-watch-<WORKSPACE_NAME>" / ps -o command= first token, not the bare agent-watch.sh which is process-indistinguishable across projects

name: HERD_REQUIRE_PROJECT_CONFIG
description: Console-strict config binding: set to 1 by the long-running consoles (agent-watch/herd-watch/backlog-view/coordinator) before sourcing herd-config.sh, so a console whose config resolves ONLY via the rule-3 engine-dogfood fallback (no HERD_CONFIG_FILE, no .herd/config walking up from $PWD) REFUSES rather than silently binding to the engine repo (issue #60); normal `herd <cmd>` CLI usage never sets it, so its intentional dogfood fallback is unchanged
when_to_surface: Internal guard the consoles set themselves; surface only when explaining why a console refused to start or how the launch-binding guard works

name: HERD_ALLOW_FOREIGN_CWD
description: Escape hatch for the launch-binding guard (issue #60): set to 1 to let a console start from a $PWD outside its resolved PROJECT_ROOT and to permit the rule-3 engine-dogfood config fallback under HERD_REQUIRE_PROJECT_CONFIG; the startup banner still prints the resolved WORKSPACE_NAME/PROJECT_ROOT
when_to_surface: When a console is intentionally launched from outside its project tree (a deliberate cross-dir or dogfood run) and the foreign-cwd/config refusal must be bypassed

name: scripts/herd/cost.sh
description: The token/cost SUMMER + PERISHABLE model→$ price table (single source of truth, dated "as of 2026-06-24"). Sums a Claude transcript's assistant-message usage (input/output/cache-read/cache-write) deduped by message id, classifies builder vs in-worktree review sessions by a stable reviewer fingerprint, prices each by model, and journals `cost` events at merge time (agent-watch.sh do_merge). Override the table via HERD_COST_PRICE_FILE (also the hermetic test seam); read the results with `herd cost`
when_to_surface: When updating pricing after an Anthropic price change or new model, verifying cost figures, or explaining where cost-per-merged-PR numbers come from — this file is the perishable price table to keep current

name: .herd-limit-sentinel
description: Per-worktree sentinel file written by the rate_limit StopFailure Claude Code hook (generated into <worktree>/.claude/settings.json by herd_write_ratelimit_hook on worktree creation) when a builder's turn ends on the account usage limit; its contents are the reset time from the banner. The watcher (agent-watch.sh _detect_limit_hit) polls it as the PRIMARY, version-robust limit-hit signal — far better than scraping the banner — then schedules an in-place `claude --continue` resume at the reset. A banner-scrape fallback (last assistant transcript line matches the usage-limit banner) covers environments where the hook is unavailable
when_to_surface: When explaining how the watcher detects a builder frozen on the usage limit, or why a builder shows a "limit-hit · auto-resume at HH:MM" row instead of "idle · no PR"

name: HERD_LIMIT_HOOK
description: Set to off to skip generating the rate_limit StopFailure hook into new worktrees (herd_write_ratelimit_hook); the watcher then relies on the banner-scrape fallback for limit detection
when_to_surface: When a project cannot use the project-level hook (unsupported Claude Code version) or wants to opt out of the generated .claude/settings.json hook

name: HERD_LIMIT_DETECT
description: Set to off to disable usage-limit detection + auto-resume entirely (kill-switch for agent-watch.sh _detect_limit_hit); default on
when_to_surface: When the limit-auto-resume feature must be turned off wholesale, e.g. debugging a false-positive limit detection

name: HERD_FORCE_SPAWN
description: Set to 1 to bypass the advisory pre-spawn review-gate saturation check (herd-spawn-gate.sh) and the no-tracker-ref spawn refusal, forcing a builder spawn through and journaling the bypass; equivalent to passing --force/-f before the slug on herd-quick.sh/herd-feature.sh. Off (default) → gates apply unchanged
when_to_surface: When an urgent item must spawn a builder despite a saturated review queue or with no tracker ref, and the operator accepts the override — the explicit escape hatch the gate points to

name: HERD_LIMIT_RESUME_BUFFER
description: Seconds to wait AFTER the parsed reset time before relaunching a limit-blocked builder via `claude --continue` (default 60); avoids racing the account quota's exact reset instant
when_to_surface: When resumes fire a hair too early and re-hit the limit — raise the buffer; the reset time itself is parsed from the banner/sentinel

name: HERD_LIMIT_UNKNOWN_WAIT
description: Seconds to hold a limit-blocked builder before resuming when the reset time could NOT be parsed (default 18000 ≈ one 5h rolling window); better to wait out a window than hammer a still-blocked account
when_to_surface: When the banner format changes and reset times stop parsing, tune how long the watcher waits before a blind resume attempt

name: COORDINATOR_WATCHDOG
description: on | off (default off): opt-in coordinator auto-resume. When on, the watcher revives a limit-parked coordinator the same way it revives builders — at the reset+buffer it relaunches the coordinator in place via `claude --continue`. Only ever acts on a CONFIRMED park (limit sentinel/banner present AND its agent not 'working'); never touches a healthy coordinator; a WORKSPACE_NAME-keyed launch lock prevents double-launch; a failed resume escalates via notification without looping. Ships DORMANT: default off, and activation also requires restarting the watcher
when_to_surface: Turn on for unattended/overnight runs so a coordinator that hits the account usage limit is auto-resumed at reset instead of stranding all orchestration until a human intervenes; leave off (default) to keep the coordinator human-managed

name: COORDINATOR_AUTONOMY
description: Coordinator spawn autonomy posture: human (default) | gated | full. human (today's byte-identical behavior) → the coordinator surfaces backlog items and waits for explicit user approval before spawning. gated → the coordinator may spawn items automatically when constraints are met (no unresolved conflicts, within budget, review capacity). full → coordinator makes all spawn decisions autonomously (for long-running unattended runs). The constraint checks — impact analysis, budget, concurrency gates — run either way; autonomy only changes whether a final human click is required. Unknown value → human. Applied on coordinator launch
when_to_surface: Set to gated or full for unattended/autonomous coordination; leave unset/human (default) for interactive operator control where each spawn requires explicit approval. Choose gated for an autonomous coordinator that still respects review bandwidth and conflict analysis, or full for a truly hands-off night/weekend run

name: DEAD_BUILDER_AUTORESPAWN
description: on | off (default off): opt-in AUTO-RESPAWN for a silently-dead pre-PR builder (agent-watch.sh). The watcher already DETECTS + surfaces a DEAD builder (worktree present, no live agent, no PR, past grace); with this on it also RESTARTS one — surgically, once. When a slug crosses into DEAD it respawns a FRESH agent in the EXISTING worktree pointed at the same $WORKTREES_DIR/<slug>.task.md (reuses the spawn pattern, NOT the full lane: no new worktree/branch, no app-preview pane). Bounded by two hard rails: it respawns ONLY when the worktree has NO commits ahead of base AND no staged/unstaged/untracked changes (never blows away work — a dead builder with work escalates instead), and AT MOST ONCE per slug via a slug-keyed ledger (a second death after the one respawn escalates, never loops). Ships DORMANT: default off and byte-inert until opted in
when_to_surface: Turn on for unattended/overnight runs so a builder whose agent silently dies before making any commits is auto-restarted in place instead of stranding an empty worktree until a human notices; leave off (default) to keep dead-builder handling detect-and-surface only

name: DEAD_GRACE_MIN
description: Minutes a builder's DEAD signature must persist before the watcher surfaces it as dead (agent-watch.sh) — long enough to cover a slow spawn-to-agent-register gap, short enough that a real death surfaces promptly. Non-numeric/unset → 2 minutes.
when_to_surface: Raise on a slow host where spawns register late; lower to surface deaths faster.

name: STALL_QUIET_MIN
description: Minutes a working builder's worktree may stay quiet before the watcher warns of a stall (agent-watch.sh). Non-numeric/unset → 5 minutes.
when_to_surface: Raise for lanes with long quiet compute phases; lower to catch stalls sooner.

name: STALE_DUP_DETECT
description: Pre-merge STALE-DUPLICATE gate (HERD-188): on (default) | off. on → the watcher's action pass runs scripts/herd/stale-dup-gate.sh BEFORE the review/merge and HOLDS (never auto-merges, surfaces a loud 🛑 needs-you row + one PR comment + notification, journals stale_dup_hold) any PR that is a PROVABLE stale duplicate — either (1) DUPLICATE: its tracked 'Refs: <ID>' item is already Done via ANOTHER merged PR carrying the same ref (the #236-vs-shipped-#185 incident that force-merged onto newer main → revert #280), or (2) STALE BASE: the files it touches were materially changed on the base branch (DEFAULT_BRANCH) by a merge this branch predates, so a clean-but-behind merge would silently clobber that newer work (deterministic git set-intersection, only when the branch is behind base). PROVABLE-ONLY + FAIL-SOFT: no item ref, an offline gh, a missing base ref, or any probe failure yields NO hold, so default-on cannot false-hold a legitimate PR. off → byte-inert (no probe, no hold, no rows). Consumed by agent-watch.sh.
when_to_surface: Leave on (default) so the watcher refuses to auto-merge work that already shipped or sits on a base a merge would silently revert; set to off to disable the gate entirely (e.g. a repo whose PRs never carry tracker refs and whose branch protection already requires up-to-date branches)

name: STALE_BASE_AUTOFIX
description: Auto-heal STALE-BASE holds from the pre-merge stale-duplicate gate (HERD-199): on | off (default off, ship-dormant). on → when the gate holds a PR for STALE-BASE (touched files moved on DEFAULT_BRANCH after the branch's merge-base), the watcher auto-bounces the live builder with a `git merge $DEFAULT_BRANCH` re-task via the EXISTING refix rails (sha-keyed once-guard kind=stale, shared REFIX_MAX_ROUNDS budget with review/health autofixes, honest console row `rebasing · awaiting push`); if there is no live builder (foreign/reaped/dead/missing pane) it dispatches the conflict resolver instead. Only bounce-exhaustion escalates to needs-you. The DUPLICATE flavor (re-implements shipped work) stays a human judgment call. off (default) → byte-identical to the pre-HERD-199 hold path (🛑 needs-you, no bounce, no ledger write). Consumed by agent-watch.sh.
when_to_surface: Enable (set on) in high-churn repos where base-stale holds fire repeatedly for purely mechanical merge-up work; leave off (default) so a stale-base hold still waits for a human. Pair with STALE_DUP_DETECT=on (the gate that produces the hold).

name: SWEEP_AUTO
description: Control-room SWEEP triggers (HERD-191): off | advise (default) | auto. The watcher runs a CHEAP debris scan (registry tabs whose slug has no worktree, dead-pid inflight markers, orphaned ppid=1 bats/healthcheck trees) on its orphan-sweep cadence (~60 s) — filesystem + kill -0 + one `ps -e`, no network. advise (default) → render one '🧹 sweep recommended: N stale tabs · M dead markers' console row and journal `sweep_advice` ONCE per distinct condition-set (an unchanged mess is silent; a changed one speaks again). auto → additionally run the SAFE legs itself: dead markers, orphan processes, PROVABLY-disposable worktrees, and registry-matched tabs (in that order — reaping frees a slug before the tab leg judges it stale). JUDGMENT legs — a worktree carrying real dirt or a closed-PR branch with unpushed unique commits — are FLAGGED WITH EVIDENCE and never auto-deleted in ANY mode, so `auto` cannot destroy unrecovered work; it also never restarts the watcher (leg 5 is CLI-only). An unrecognized value degrades to advise, never to auto. off → byte-inert: no scan, no row, no journal. Consumed by agent-watch.sh via sweep.sh
when_to_surface: Leave at advise (default) for a quiet nudge when debris accumulates; set auto in a long-running control room that should self-clean the provably-safe debris between sessions; set off for a byte-identical console. Watcher-affecting (takes effect on the next watcher restart). Run `herd sweep` for the full on-demand cleanup including the judgment findings and the watcher restart

name: scripts/herd/layout-reconcile.sh
description: The shared EYES-ON-LAYOUT helper. Classifies every pane in a tab by its live foreground process (herdr pane list + process-info → backlog|watch|agent|bare|busy|gone), then layout_snapshot scans a tab, layout_reconcile adopts live role panes / flags duplicate viewers / names missing roles (registry pane-ids trusted only when still observed — neutralising a stale or poisoned .herd-panes), layout_write_registry rewrites the registry from OBSERVED state, and layout_fold_stray_tabs closes standalone watch-/backlog- tabs. Sourced by bin/herd (cmd_reload + herd pane) and coordinator.sh so every pane-mutating path observes reality before it mutates
when_to_surface: When changing how the control room resolves/creates/adopts panes, debugging a duplicate viewer or a reload that built standalone tabs, or explaining why a mutating path observes the live layout before trusting the registry

name: TASK_PANE_VIEW
description: Builder-tab task-spec viewer: on (default) | off. When on, the builder lanes (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh) render the builder's live task spec ($WORKTREES_DIR/<slug>.task.md) in the tab's OTHERWISE-IDLE root pane via task-spec-view.sh (glow + the bundled tokyonight style, repaint-on-mtime, a quiet dim 'task spec removed' line when the file is reaped — never red), so a human sees WHAT the agent was told to build instead of a bare shell. Strictly ADDITIVE UX: the pane is unused today, so off (default on) restores the bare shell exactly. Launched ONLY when the root pane is not hosting the app preview or any live process, and NEVER under the headless driver (no panes → clean no-op). Applied on the next builder spawn.
when_to_surface: Set to off to keep builder tabs' root pane a bare shell; leave on (default) to surface each builder's task spec live in its otherwise-unused root pane

name: DOCTOR_STARTUP_HINT
description: Proactive soft-dependency surfacing on control-room startup (herd reload / coordinator launch): off (default) | on. Default off prints NOTHING extra, so every startup path is byte-identical unless opted in. When on, startup emits one DIM line per MISSING soft dep (glow -> raw backlog pane, shellcheck -> no lint, bats -> direct test run), each naming the single feature it degrades, then a dim pointer to `herd doctor` for the install command. Never red, never blocks (soft deps only degrade; the no-false-red rule). Applied on the next herd reload / coordinator launch
when_to_surface: Set to on to be reminded at control-room startup which optional tools are missing and what each degrades, instead of discovering it when the degradation bites; leave off (default) for a byte-identical startup

name: AGENT_UPDATE
description: Opt-in safe self-update of the AGENT RUNTIME (HERD-149): off (default) | on. Default off runs NOTHING — no installer probe, no update exec, no xattr — so the tree is byte-identical and operators keep updating claude (or, via the driver seam, codex/grok) by hand or a personal OS job, outside the engine. When on, `herd agent-update` (scripts/herd/agent-update.sh) detects the installer (brew/npm/native) of the runtime HERD_DRIVER points at, runs the update, and — the whole point — de-quarantines the resolved binary on macOS (strips com.apple.quarantine, the issue #137 footgun where a `brew upgrade --cask` leaves the new binary quarantined so every new exec hangs in _dyld_start). DRIVER-AWARE (each runtime-specific value comes from the active driver's DRIVER_AGENT_* bindings) and FAIL-SOFT (a missing runtime / failed installer warns, never hard-aborts). Any value other than on is treated as off
when_to_surface: Set to on to have the engine keep the agent runtime current (and auto-handle the macOS cask-quarantine footgun) instead of a hand-run upgrade; leave off (default) for byte-identical behavior. Preview with `herd agent-update --dry-run`

name: ENGINE_MIN
description: The ENGINE VERSION HANDSHAKE floor (HERD-179): the minimum herdkit ENGINE LEVEL this project requires, committed in .herd/config. `herd upgrade` STAMPS it to the level of the engine that ran the upgrade and only ever RAISES it (monotonic), so an operator on an older checkout cannot silently un-pin the project. An engine below the floor is STALE and every WRITE path refuses it with one remedy — `run herd update`: lane spawn preflight (herd-preflight.sh), herd-claim, scribe-step apply (commit/add-item/update-state/amend), and `herd backend switch`. READ paths only warn (`herd status`, the advisory `herd doctor` row). HERD_ENGINE_SKIP_HANDSHAKE=1 is the deliberate escape hatch: it downgrades any refusal to a warning and JOURNALS the bypass (engine_handshake_bypass), so a forced write is always recoverable from `herd log`. Default 0 / absent ⇒ no floor ⇒ the handshake is inert and behavior is byte-identical to before it existed. The engine's own level constant (_HERD_ENGINE_LEVEL) lives in scripts/herd/engine-version.sh
when_to_surface: When a second operator (or a second machine) can run an OLDER engine checkout against this project's committed config — the handshake stops that stale engine from claiming an item, spawning a lane, draining a scribe request, or switching the backend. You never set it by hand; `herd upgrade` stamps it

name: ENGINE_AUTOUPDATE
description: What the engine DOES about a stale local checkout (HERD-179): off (default) | check | auto. off runs nothing beyond the always-advisory `herd doctor` engine row. check paints one quiet 'engine outdated' note on the watcher console (byte-identical console otherwise) and calls it out in the doctor; `herd update` stays a manual act. auto additionally has the watcher dispatch `herd update` in a QUIESCENT window every ~5 min — reusing that command's OWN refusal (it aborts under HERD_NONINTERACTIVE while builders are mid-flight, since their lanes reference engine scripts live, or over a dirty/diverged engine checkout) and rate-limited by a cooldown (HERD_ENGINE_COOLDOWN_SECS, default 900) so a persistent refusal never hammers the remote. The update is dispatched DETACHED because `herd update` ends in a reload that restarts the watcher. Any value other than check/auto is off
when_to_surface: Set to check when you want the console + doctor to tell you the engine has fallen behind this project's ENGINE_MIN; set to auto when you want the watcher to pull the engine forward for you between builds. Leave off (default) to keep `herd update` entirely manual

name: scripts/herd/task-spec-view.sh
description: The builder-tab task-spec viewer. Renders a builder's live task spec ($WORKTREES_DIR/<slug>.task.md) into the tab's otherwise-idle ROOT pane: glow + the bundled tokyonight style (else glow -s dark, else cat) painted directly to the pane TTY, repainting only on the file's mtime change, and showing one quiet dim 'task spec removed' line (no red, per the no-false-red rule) when the file is reaped with the worktree/tab — never crashing. Launched by herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh via the driver send-text surface, gated on TASK_PANE_VIEW (default on) and only when the root pane is unused; a no-op under the headless driver. Modeled on backlog-view.sh's file loop.
when_to_surface: When changing how a builder tab surfaces its task spec, or debugging why a builder tab's root pane shows/does not show the live task spec

name: HERD_THEME
description: Pluggable theming across all herd color surfaces: name of the active theme (default: tokyonight, the shipped built-in). A theme is a DIRECTORY holding palette.sh (console C_* truecolor + optional C_CLI_* 16-color CLI overrides) and glow.json (the glamour style). Resolution order per file: .herd/themes/<name>/ (user, project-local) -> templates/themes/<name>/ (engine built-ins) -> tokyonight fallback. An unknown/broken theme warns loudly once and falls back, never breaking a console. Threaded through agent-watch.sh (console palette), backlog-view.sh + task-spec-view.sh (glow.json), and bin/herd status/fleet/cost/why + herd-approve.sh (CLI palette). Fails soft: missing theme file -> built-in default; NO_COLOR or non-TTY renders plain. Default tokyonight is byte-identical to the pre-theme hardcoded palettes
when_to_surface: Set to switch every herd surface to a different built-in theme, or to a user theme dropped in .herd/themes/<name>/ (palette.sh + glow.json); leave unset/tokyonight for the shipped default rendering

name: scripts/herd/theme.sh
description: The HERD_THEME resolver library, sourced by every color surface. Resolves a theme dir palette.sh + glow.json via the search order (.herd/themes/<name>/ -> templates/themes/<name>/ -> tokyonight), fails soft to the built-in default (warns once on an unknown/broken theme), and blanks colors under NO_COLOR / a non-TTY stdout. herd_theme_load_console sets the truecolor C_* console palette; herd_theme_load_cli sets the 16-color c_* CLI palette (preferring C_CLI_*, falling back to C_*); herd_theme_glow_style echoes the resolved glow.json path
when_to_surface: When adding a new color-emitting surface (source theme.sh and call herd_theme_load_console / herd_theme_load_cli / herd_theme_glow_style), authoring a custom theme, or debugging why a surface did or did not pick up a theme

name: .herd/themes
description: User-defined, project-local theme directories: .herd/themes/<name>/ holding palette.sh + glow.json. Highest-precedence resolution for HERD_THEME=<name> (overrides an engine built-in of the same name). Read at render time; committing it is optional
when_to_surface: When a project wants its own color theme: drop palette.sh + glow.json into .herd/themes/<name>/ and set HERD_THEME=<name>

name: CLAIM_REQUIRED
description: Atomic work-item claiming gate (HERD-50): when on, the builder lanes (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh) run a SYNCHRONOUS claim via scripts/herd/herd-claim.sh BEFORE creating a worktree — but ONLY when a tracker id is present (HERD_CLAIM_ID, else the HERD_ITEM_REF the coordinator threads for tracked items). The claim reads the item CURRENT state+assignee through the active SCRIBE_BACKEND _backend_claim_item op, sets it In Progress + assigned to the operator identity (WATCHER_OWNER, else gh api user), and RE-READS to verify. An item already claimed by ANOTHER identity ABORTS the spawn loudly (no worktree, no agent, non-zero exit). OFF by default → today behavior exactly (no claim; the async scribe mark-in-progress is the only state write). No-id/unclaimed spawns pass through unchanged; a backend that is unreachable FAILS SOFT (warn + proceed) so a solo operator is never hard-blocked. Linear/GitHub lack compare-and-swap so claim-verify NARROWS the race to seconds; the file backend flip is atomic via git push serialization (loser push rejected, re-pull shows claimed, abort)
when_to_surface: Set to on when two or more operators work one repo concurrently and you need to stop them double-building the same backlog item; leave off (default) for a single operator or byte-identical legacy behavior. Requires a tracker id on the spawn (HERD_CLAIM_ID or HERD_ITEM_REF) to activate

name: TRACKED_SPAWNS
description: Tracker-routed spawn enforcement (HERD-64): make "every builder is traceable to a tracked work item" a PROJECT POLICY rather than an operator convention. off (default) | required. off → today's behavior EXACTLY: the spawn surfaces (herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh / spawn.sh) impose no gate. required → a spawn carrying NO tracker ref (HERD_CLAIM_ID, else the HERD_ITEM_REF the coordinator threads for tracked items) is REFUSED with a loud one-line reason, creating nothing; HERD_FORCE_SPAWN=1 (or a lane's --force) is the explicit escape hatch that lets it through and JOURNALS the bypass (tracked_spawn_bypassed). Enforced via herd_tracked_spawn_or_abort (herd-config.sh); the durable spawn queue threads the ref through a sidecar so a queued tracked intent is never re-refused at drain time. Any value but required is treated as off. Composes with CLAIM_REQUIRED (HERD-50): the ref set is identical, so with both on every spawn is atomically CLAIMED and VISIBLE in the tracker — TRACKED_SPAWNS enforces visibility, CLAIM_REQUIRED exclusivity
when_to_surface: Set to required on a shared repo (or any project that wants an auditable tracker trail) so no builder is spawned off-book — every worktree maps to a tracked item; leave off (default) for a single operator or byte-identical legacy behavior. Pairs with CLAIM_REQUIRED for multi-operator repos

name: herd-claim.sh
description: Atomic pre-spawn work-item claim (HERD-50): sourced by herd-quick.sh / herd-feature.sh and called ONCE before worktree creation. herd_claim_or_abort resolves the tracker id (HERD_CLAIM_ID else HERD_ITEM_REF) and operator identity (WATCHER_OWNER else gh api user), and — only when CLAIM_REQUIRED is on and an id is present — claims the item synchronously through the active SCRIBE_BACKEND _backend_claim_item op (sourced in a subshell so backend helpers never leak). Returns non-zero to ABORT the spawn when the item is already claimed by another identity; returns 0 (proceed) for our-own-claim, self-claim, no-id/disabled, and fail-soft backend-unreachable paths
when_to_surface: Internal to the builder lanes; not launched directly. Governs whether a spawn claims its tracked item before building, gated by CLAIM_REQUIRED

name: MCP_PROVISION
description: Builder MCP tool-provisioning surface (the tools SIBLING of CONTEXT_PROVISION): a SPACE-SEPARATED list of MCP server names wired into every spawned builder worktree's project-level .claude/settings.json (mcpServers block) by herd_write_mcp_servers (herd-config.sh), called from new-feature.sh, so a builder can reach those tools with no per-session setup. Empty/unset (default) → NOTHING written, settings.json byte-identical to today. The write is ADDITIVE + NON-CLOBBER (per-entry: an existing/user-authored server of the same name is never overwritten), idempotent, and atomic — same guarantees as the rate-limit hook it merges alongside. Each name resolves to a {command,args,env} entry from a BUILT-IN default, overridable per server by MCP_<NAME>_COMMAND / MCP_<NAME>_ARGS / MCP_<NAME>_ENV (NAME upper-cased, -/. → _), set by editing .herd/config directly (dynamic per-server keys are not registered here). A name with no built-in and no _COMMAND override is ignored (forward-compatible). PRIVACY: credentials are NEVER written — MCP_<NAME>_ENV lists env-var NAMES passed through as "${VAR}" references Claude Code expands at launch from the runtime env (populated from .herd/secrets), so no secret value lands in any committed/generated file; DENY_PATHS stays honored. Built-ins: context7 (up-to-date library docs, high-value for CONSUMER projects; low-value in bash herdkit itself — the mechanism is the deliverable) and graphify-mcp (local example server)
when_to_surface: Set to e.g. 'context7' to wire the up-to-date-library-docs MCP server into each builder worktree so builders can query live library docs (most valuable on real code CONSUMER projects herdkit runs on); leave empty (default) for today's byte-identical settings.json with no MCP wiring. Space-separate multiple servers; override a server's command/args/env or add a custom server via MCP_<NAME>_COMMAND/_ARGS/_ENV in .herd/config

name: GRAPHIFY_BIN
description: Machine-local path to the graphify call-graph binary (PyPI 'graphifyy'; usually ~/.local/bin/graphify) that the COORDINATOR resolves for its pre-spawn/periodic impact-conflict analysis — the coordinator-side THIRD LEG alongside CONTEXT_PROVISION=codemap (builder grounding) and MCP_PROVISION graphify-mcp (builder tools). Resolution: this key, else 'graphify' on PATH; when NEITHER resolves the analysis is SKIPPED SILENTLY (no warning, no red row — the tool is an optional accelerator, never required). MACHINE-scoped (HERD-47): the path is per-machine, so 'herd config set GRAPHIFY_BIN <path>' routes to the gitignored .herd/config.local, never the committed baseline. The coordinator runs 'graphify update --no-cluster' to rebuild graphify-out/graph.json LLM-free (zero Claude quota), then crosses each in-flight worktree's changed files against the other worktrees AND the candidate item's likely file surface to sequence/warn on overlap before spawning. HONEST SCOPE: FILE-level overlap + within-file structure ONLY — graphify's bash cross-file CALL resolution is weak (measured 2026-07-06: 0 cross-file call links among the big engine scripts; the ~136 repo-wide cross-file links are mostly imports/contains), so this is NOT a cross-script call graph
when_to_surface: Set to the absolute path of the graphify binary on THIS machine (e.g. ~/.local/bin/graphify) to enable the coordinator's pre-spawn overlap read; leave unset to fall back to 'graphify' on PATH, and if that is absent too the analysis is skipped silently (optional — the coordinator still spawns normally)

name: .herd/config.local
description: Per-user / per-machine overlay for .herd/config (gitignored, HERD-47): sourced AFTER .herd/config by herd-config.sh so its keys OVERRIDE the committed baseline, mirroring the settings.json/settings.local.json convention. Absent => effective config is byte-identical to a single-file setup (backward-compatible). Write to it with `herd config set --local KEY VALUE`; every key marked scope=machine in this manifest routes there by DEFAULT even without --local — currently: COORDINATOR_AUTONOMY, COORDINATOR_WATCHDOG, DEAD_BUILDER_AUTORESPAWN, GRAPHIFY_BIN, HERD_DRIVER, INFRA_BREAKER_COOLDOWN, INFRA_BREAKER_MAX, MODEL_ADVISE, MODEL_COORDINATOR, MODEL_FEATURE, MODEL_QUICK, MODEL_RESEARCH, MODEL_RESOLVER, MODEL_REVIEW, MODEL_SCRIBE, REVIEW_MODEL_ESCALATED, TOKEN_MODE, WATCHER_OWNER, WATCHER_VIEW, WATCHER_VIEW_ASSIGNEE, WATCHER_VIEW_AUTHOR, WATCHER_VIEW_DEPS_LABEL, WATCHER_VIEW_LABEL, WATCHER_VIEW_STATUS, WATCH_CLAUDE_PROBE_TIMEOUT, WORKSPACE_NAME. `herd config list` shows each value source (baseline | local). ZERO-SECRET: credentials stay in .herd/secrets
when_to_surface: Keep per-user/per-machine state (driver, model-tier & token-budget choices, operator-posture opt-ins) out of the shared committed baseline so a collaborator edit never diverges main or dirties the ff-only `herd update` pull

name: .herd/steps.tsv
description: Declarative PIPELINE STEP LIST (HERD-132), committed like .herd/links: one TAB-separated row per custom stage — name | at (post-build|post-healthcheck|pre-merge|post-merge) | run (shell command or skill:<name>) | on_fail (block|warn) | hold (none|notify|approve). Blank / '#'-prefixed lines ignored. Validated by 'steps.sh validate'; consumed by scripts/herd/steps.sh. Absent/empty ⇒ byte-identical pipeline (feature ships dormant)

name: .herd/triggers.tsv
description: Declarative SCHEDULED/TRIGGERED RUN LIST (HERD-169), committed like .herd/steps.tsv: one TAB-separated row per trigger — name | schedule (@hourly|@daily|@weekly|every:<N>[smhd]|manual) | lane (quick|feature) | task (workflow text; {item}/{name} tokens) | input (shell command whose stdout lines are the input set). Blank / '#'-prefixed lines ignored. Validated by 'triggers.sh validate'; consumed by scripts/herd/triggers.sh, which diffs the input set against .herd/trigger-state/<name>.snapshot and enqueues one spawn per new line. Absent/empty ⇒ byte-identical engine (feature ships dormant)

name: templates/postures.tsv
description: The CANONICAL CONFIG POSTURES (HERD-153): the committed, authoritative set of named operating postures — solo-auto (MERGE_POLICY=auto) | team-approve (MERGE_POLICY=approve + HUMAN_VERIFY_POLICY=hold) | gated-push (PUSH_GATE=human + PR_FLOW=draft) | custom-steps (a .herd/steps.tsv approve-stage) | observe-only (MERGE_POLICY=observe). Each posture is a small named bundle of .herd/config KEY=VALUE settings (name | keys | intent, TAB-separated). SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH consumed by the posture-matrix sim (scripts/herd/sim/sandbox-posture-matrix.sh, which proves the gate loop under each posture) and by HERD-141 init v2 posture profiles (queued) to offer + render these bundles at herd init time. Add a posture here, not in the sim or installer, and every consumer picks it up
when_to_surface: When choosing or documenting a project's governance stance at init, or when adding a new named posture bundle — edit templates/postures.tsv (authoritative), then the posture-matrix sim and HERD-141 both consume it

name: herd conformance <report|run>
description: The capability CONFORMANCE MATRIX (HERD-144): joins the capabilities manifest (templates/capabilities.tsv) against the committed proof map (templates/conformance.tsv, a SIBLING keyed by capability name so the manifest stays byte-identical) to make per-feature test coverage accountable. `report` [--json] is DETERMINISTIC + no-LLM: it lists every capability with NO proof mapping (a gap) and every mapping whose proof_ref no longer exists (rot), exiting non-zero ONLY on rot (a dangling ref — gaps alone never fail). `run` [--kind unit] executes the mapped proofs and writes conformance.json (capability -> verdict -> sha -> timestamp), the whole-surface analog of the sim scorecard. FAIL-SOFT: an absent proof map is a soft note (every capability reads as a gap), never a red row; REPORT-ONLY — nothing here is a merge gate.
when_to_surface: When auditing which capabilities have or lack test coverage, after adding a capability or a test so the proof map stays honest, or to catch a proof_ref a rename/deletion left dangling; run `herd conformance report` for the gap/rot list and `herd conformance run` to execute mapped proofs into conformance.json

name: MODEL_* ref <driver>:<model>
description: Runtime-qualified MODEL_* value (HERD-151): every MODEL_* key accepts an optionally driver-qualified ref '<driver>:<model>' pinning a role's agent to a shipped runtime driver (templates/drivers/<name>.driver) AND model, not just Claude. A BARE value (no '<driver>:' prefix) resolves to the default driver with the whole value as the model, so every existing bare-claude config is BYTE-IDENTICAL. Resolved at spawn time by scripts/herd/driver.sh (herd_model_resolve/herd_model_for_spawn) — the seam next to PR #264's DRIVER_AGENT_* exec bindings; an UNKNOWN driver prefix is a LOUD hard error at spawn, never a silent claude fallback. The bare model id is what the runtime spawns on --model; the resolved driver feeds the queued HERD-150 P2-P5 runtime routing
when_to_surface: When pointing a role (feature/quick/review/scribe/research/resolver/coordinator/advise) at a non-default runtime, or reading why a MODEL_* value with a colon fails loudly at spawn — write a bare model for today's default-driver behavior, or <driver>:<model> naming a shipped driver

name: templates/models.tsv
description: Model SUGGESTIONS CATALOG (HERD-151): an advisory, perishable TSV of model refs with a coarse tier (judgment|balanced|cheap|docs), a role-fit hint and an as-of date. 'herd config set MODEL_*' consults it to SUGGEST a fit and to soft-note an uncatalogued model or an unshipped driver prefix — it NEVER refuses an operator's model (catalog / CLAUDE.md / manual set are coequal, none required). Not a gate: a stale or missing row never blocks a spawn or a merge. Browse with 'herd config models'; validate rows with 'herd config models lint' (tier + date per row). Keyed by the bare model id; price a catalogued foreign model in cost.sh too, else it shows as 'unpriced' in herd cost
when_to_surface: When choosing a MODEL_* value, or curating the recommended model set for a project; extend it (bump each row's as_of) as runtimes ship new models

name: herd config models
description: Print the model suggestions catalog (templates/models.tsv) — each ref with its tier, role-fit hint and as-of date. 'herd config models lint' validates every row (4 columns, tier in judgment|balanced|cheap|docs, as_of shaped YYYY-MM-DD) and exits non-zero on any malformed row. Read-only; advisory (never a gate)
when_to_surface: Browsing recommended models before 'herd config set MODEL_*', or checking the catalog is well-formed after editing templates/models.tsv
